#endif

functions.longDescription: C23 adds bit-precise integer types. Confirm compiler support before using them in portable projects.

functions.longDescription: Use size_t for object sizes and ptrdiff_t for pointer differences.

functions.longDescription: Use exact-width integer types for binary formats and register fields.

functions.name: _Generic type-based dispatch
functions.description: Choose an implementation at compile time from the argument type.
functions.longDescription: _Generic is the C11 compile-time selection mechanism. It is the closest thing C has to overload resolution and is useful for typed macros, math wrappers, and API front-ends that keep a C call surface.

functions.name: X-macro + _Generic typed API front-end
functions.signature:
#define SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(X)     X(int, int)                     X(double, double)

#define DECLARE_PUSH(typeName, cType)     void Push_##typeName(cType value);
SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(DECLARE_PUSH)

#define SENSOR_PUSH(value) _Generic((value),     int: Push_int,     double: Push_double )(value)
functions.insertText:
#define SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(X)     X(int, int)                     X(double, double)

#define DECLARE_PUSH(typeName, cType)     void Push_##typeName(cType value);
SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(DECLARE_PUSH)

#define SENSOR_PUSH(value) _Generic((value),     int: Push_int,     double: Push_double )(value)
functions.description: Generate a family of typed entry points and one generic dispatch macro.
functions.longDescription: This pattern keeps one master type list and uses it twice: once to generate the declarations and once to build the _Generic dispatch. It scales better than hand-writing many similar wrappers.
functions.declaration:
#define SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(X)     X(int, int)                     X(double, double)

#define DECLARE_PUSH(typeName, cType)     void Push_##typeName(cType value);
SENSOR_NUMERIC_TABLE(DECLARE_PUSH)

#define SENSOR_PUSH(value) _Generic((value),     int: Push_int,     double: Push_double )(value)

functions.name: _Alignas / _Alignof
functions.description: Control and query alignment explicitly in C11.
functions.longDescription: Explicit alignment matters for SIMD, DMA buffers, lock-free structures, and ABI compatibility. _Alignas/_Alignof are the core language facilities in C11, while stdalign.h also provides the alignas/alignof convenience macros on supporting implementations.

functions.name: _Static_assert ABI contract
functions.description: Stop the build when a type layout no longer matches the binary contract.
functions.longDescription: Binary interfaces should fail fast when structure size or field offsets drift. _Static_assert is one of the most effective tools for locking down file formats, IPC packets, driver descriptors, and shared-library ABIs.

functions.name: bit fields for packed flags
functions.description: Store small integral fields inside one machine word.
functions.longDescription: Bit fields are compact, but their layout, signedness details, and packing can be implementation-defined. They are useful for local flags and documentation, but raw protocol or register layouts often still need explicit masks and shifts for predictable ABI control.

functions.name: endianness explicit encode decode
functions.description: Serialize integers explicitly instead of relying on host layout.
functions.longDescription: Serialize integers explicitly instead of relying on host layout.

functions.name: flexible array member packet
functions.description: Attach variable-length trailing storage to a structure.
functions.longDescription: A flexible array member lets one allocation hold both the fixed header and the trailing variable-sized data. This is an important systems-programming and ABI pattern for binary messages, IPC blocks, and custom allocators.

functions.name: offsetof container field
functions.description: Query a member offset for ABI checks or intrusive structures.
functions.longDescription: Query a member offset for ABI checks or intrusive structures.

functions.name: tagged union state machine payload
functions.description: Represent one of several payload shapes with an explicit discriminator.
functions.longDescription: A tagged union is the canonical C representation for a sum type. The discriminator tells the reader which union member is currently valid. This pattern is common in protocol parsers, UI events, and device state machines.

functions.name: union for binary reinterpretation with memcpy alternative
functions.description: Overlay representations carefully and prefer memcpy for portable type punning.
functions.longDescription: Unions are often used for binary inspection, but aliasing and active-member rules are subtle across standards and compilers. When the goal is only to copy raw representation bytes, memcpy is the conservative portable technique.

functions.name: designated initializer
functions.description: Initialize selected structure fields by name.
functions.longDescription: Initialize selected structure fields by name.

functions.name: enum state machine
functions.description: Represent discrete states with an enumeration.
functions.longDescription: Represent discrete states with an enumeration.

functions.name: struct deep copy with heap field
functions.description: Duplicate pointed data explicitly when a C struct owns heap memory.
functions.longDescription: This is a deep copy because the destination receives its own independently allocated character buffer instead of aliasing src->name.

functions.name: struct shallow copy
functions.description: Copy a structure by value in C.
functions.longDescription: A plain assignment copies each field bit-for-bit. Scalar fields are duplicated, but pointer fields still point to the same underlying memory.

functions.name: array length macro
functions.description: Compute the element count of a real array, not a pointer.
functions.longDescription: Compute the element count of a real array, not a pointer.

functions.name: array parameter decay note
functions.description: Pass the array and its count explicitly because function parameters decay to pointers.
functions.longDescription: Pass the array and its count explicitly because function parameters decay to pointers.

functions.name: all four const pointer forms
functions.description: Compare mutable pointers, pointers to const, const pointers, and const pointers to const.
functions.longDescription: Compare mutable pointers, pointers to const, const pointers, and const pointers to const.

functions.name: void pointer round trip
functions.description: Use void * as an opaque object pointer and cast back to the original type.
functions.longDescription: Use void * as an opaque object pointer and cast back to the original type.

functions.name: container_of intrusive structure
functions.description: Recover the outer object from the address of an embedded node.
functions.longDescription: container_of is a classic intrusive-data-structure macro used heavily in kernels, runtimes, and embedded frameworks. It depends on a stable memory layout and should be paired with disciplined ownership and lifetime rules.

functions.name: double pointer out parameter
functions.description: Return allocated storage through a T ** out parameter.
functions.longDescription: A T ** out parameter is the standard C pattern when the callee must allocate or reseat a caller-owned pointer. It is also common for handle factories, DLL boundaries, and functions that grow a buffer dynamically.

functions.name: pointer to const vs const pointer
functions.description: Distinguish read-only pointees from fixed pointer variables.
functions.longDescription: In C declarations, const binds to what is immediately on its left when possible. pointer-to-const protects the pointed value, while const-pointer freezes the pointer variable itself. This distinction is fundamental when reading API signatures and callback parameters.

functions.name: restrict-qualified pointer parameters
functions.description: Promise non-overlapping pointed ranges to enable better optimization.
functions.longDescription: restrict is a C contract saying that, for the lifetime of the restricted access, the pointed object is reached through that pointer expression family only. When the promise is true, the optimizer can vectorize and reorder more aggressively. When it is false, behavior is undefined.

functions.name: strict aliasing safe type punning with memcpy
functions.description: Read raw bytes as another scalar type without violating aliasing assumptions.
functions.longDescription: Casting one unrelated pointer type to another and dereferencing it can violate strict aliasing rules. memcpy communicates a representation copy instead of an aliasing relationship, which keeps the code portable and optimization-friendly.

functions.name: memmove overlapping ranges
functions.signature:
#include <string.h>

memmove(&buffer[0], &buffer[1], (count - 1U) * sizeof(buffer[0]));
functions.description: Use memmove when source and destination ranges can overlap.
functions.longDescription: Use memmove when source and destination ranges can overlap.
functions.declaration:
#include <string.h>

memmove(&buffer[0], &buffer[1], (count - 1U) * sizeof(buffer[0]));

functions.name: snprintf bounded formatting
functions.description: Format text into a fixed-size buffer and verify truncation.
functions.longDescription: Format text into a fixed-size buffer and verify truncation.

functions.name: strtok_r POSIX tokenization
functions.description: Tokenize mutable text reentrantly on POSIX systems.
functions.longDescription: Tokenize mutable text reentrantly on POSIX systems.

functions.name: strtol checked integer parse
functions.description: Parse an integer and validate errors, trailing text, and range.
functions.longDescription: Parse an integer and validate errors, trailing text, and range.

functions.name: callback with user context
functions.description: Pass both a function pointer and an opaque context pointer through a C API.
functions.longDescription: The callback-plus-context pattern is the workhorse of C APIs because it preserves state without globals and stays ABI-friendly across DLL boundaries. It is the standard answer when a callback needs object-like state.

functions.name: function table pseudo-vtable
functions.description: Bundle a set of operations into one struct of function pointers.
functions.longDescription: A function table is the C equivalent of a vtable. It is useful for hardware abstraction layers, test doubles, plugins, and protocol backends because behavior can be swapped without changing the public object layout.

functions.name: qsort-style comparator + context thunk
functions.description: Adapt a stateful comparison into a plain C callback API.
functions.longDescription: Many legacy C APIs accept a bare function pointer with no user data. A thunk or adapter layer is then needed to bridge toward a stateful comparator. On platforms that provide qsort_r or qsort_s, prefer the native context-aware variant when available.

functions.name: Win32 WNDPROC with user data in C
functions.description: Store a typed context pointer in GWLP_USERDATA and recover it in the window procedure.
functions.longDescription: Win32 often passes control through a C callback boundary. GWLP_USERDATA is the normal slot used to associate a typed application context with one window instance so the callback can recover object-like state.

functions.name: callback registration with user data
functions.description: Store a callback and opaque context for later notification.
functions.longDescription: Store a callback and opaque context for later notification.

functions.name: function prototype and definition
functions.description: Declare a function in a header and define it in one source file.
functions.longDescription: Declare a function in a header and define it in one source file.

functions.name: static inline header helper
functions.description: Define a small header helper with internal linkage semantics.
functions.longDescription: Define a small header helper with internal linkage semantics.

functions.name: array of function pointers
functions.description: Dispatch through a small table of C function pointers.
functions.longDescription: Arrays or structs of function pointers are the standard C technique for command tables, protocol handlers, and pseudo-object interfaces.

functions.name: CVI callbackData cast
functions.description: Read custom context passed through a LabWindows/CVI callback.
functions.longDescription: In CVI, callbackData is an opaque user pointer supplied when wiring the callback. Casting it back to a dedicated context struct is the clean way to avoid globals and still access per-panel or per-control state.

functions.name: Function pointer callback
functions.signature:
typedef void (*MyCallback)(int code, void *userData);

void RegisterCallback(MyCallback callback, void *userData);
functions.insertText:
typedef void (*MyCallback)(int code, void *userData);

void RegisterCallback(MyCallback callback, void *userData);
functions.description: Callback typedef and registration example in C.
functions.longDescription: Useful in embedded software, DLL exports, or event-driven APIs.
functions.declaration:
typedef void (*MyCallback)(int code, void *userData);

void RegisterCallback(MyCallback callback, void *userData);

functions.name: function pointer declaration
functions.description: Declare a plain C function pointer and assign a compatible function to it.
functions.longDescription: A function pointer stores the address of a compatible function. This is the core mechanism behind callbacks, dispatch tables, plugin hooks, interrupt vectors, and many C APIs.

functions.name: function pointer parameter + context
functions.description: Pass a callback and an opaque context through a regular C function.
functions.longDescription: This is the smallest reusable callback API shape in C: a plain function pointer plus a void * context. It scales from simple loops to drivers, protocol decoders, GUI hooks, and asynchronous notification APIs.

functions.name: function returning function pointer with typedef
functions.description: Use typedef to keep function-pointer return types readable.
functions.longDescription: Raw declarations for functions returning function pointers become hard to read quickly. A typedef keeps state-machine handlers, ISR tables, and dispatch factories maintainable.

functions.name: InstallCtrlCallback with callback data
functions.description: Register a CVI control callback and pass a typed user context.
functions.longDescription: CVI callbacks become much easier to scale when the panel or control state lives in a context struct passed through callbackData. This avoids hidden globals and keeps multiple panels or devices independent.

functions.name: void * user context cast in callback
functions.description: Recover typed state from an opaque callbackData or userData pointer.
functions.longDescription: Many C APIs, including GUI and instrumentation frameworks, pass a generic void * callbackData so the callback can recover caller-owned state. The normal pattern is to cast the opaque pointer back to the expected struct type at the start of the callback.

functions.name: dispatch table interface
functions.description: Represent a driver interface with function pointers and an opaque instance pointer.
functions.longDescription: Represent a driver interface with function pointers and an opaque instance pointer.

functions.name: sentinel-terminated variadic function
functions.description: Stop reading variadic arguments when a NULL sentinel is reached.
functions.longDescription: Some C APIs use a sentinel instead of an explicit count. This is flexible, but it is also fragile because the caller must remember to append the terminator with the correct type.

functions.name: va_copy reuse argument list
functions.description: Duplicate a va_list before traversing it more than once.
functions.longDescription: A va_list is a consumable cursor, not a rewindable container. If you need a second pass, copy it first with va_copy and then end both lists separately.

functions.name: va_list + va_start + va_arg + va_end
functions.description: Consume a variadic argument list with the stdarg API.
functions.longDescription: This is the core C variadic-function pattern. The fixed parameter count tells the callee how many promoted arguments to read. The extracted type passed to va_arg must exactly match what the caller supplied after the default promotions.

functions.name: variadic function declaration (...)
functions.signature: int LogMessage(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...);
functions.description: Declare a C function with a fixed prefix and a variadic tail.
functions.longDescription: In C, a variadic function must declare at least one fixed parameter before the ellipsis. The caller and callee then agree on a convention such as a format string, an explicit count, or a sentinel terminator.
functions.declaration: int LogMessage(const char *level, const char *fmt, ...);

functions.name: variadic wrapper forwarding to vfprintf
functions.description: Forward a ... argument list to a v* stdio function.
functions.longDescription: When a function accepts ... and then delegates to printf-family formatting, the correct target is the matching v* API such as vprintf, vfprintf, vsnprintf, or vscanf. You cannot forward ... directly without first materializing a va_list.

functions.name: simple arena allocator
functions.description: Serve many short-lived allocations from one linear arena.
functions.longDescription: Serve many short-lived allocations from one linear arena.

functions.name: calloc / realloc
functions.description: Allocate zeroed memory and resize it safely.
functions.longDescription: calloc initializes memory to zero while realloc resizes an existing allocation. Keep realloc failure handling explicit when the original pointer must stay valid.

functions.name: malloc / free
functions.description: Dynamic allocation pattern in C.
functions.longDescription: Classic heap allocation in C. Always check the returned pointer and free the memory exactly once.

functions.name: malloc array with overflow check
functions.description: Check multiplication overflow before allocating an array.
functions.longDescription: Check multiplication overflow before allocating an array.

functions.name: safe realloc temporary pointer
functions.description: Keep the original allocation valid when realloc fails.
functions.longDescription: Keep the original allocation valid when realloc fails.

functions.name: zero initialize aggregate
functions.signature: Config config = {0};
functions.description: Initialize every aggregate field to zero.
functions.longDescription: Initialize every aggregate field to zero.
functions.declaration: Config config = {0};

functions.name: compound literal temporary object
functions.description: Create an unnamed object inline and pass its address immediately.
functions.longDescription: Compound literals are an underrated C feature. They provide a concise way to build one-shot objects for configuration calls, tests, or table entries without first declaring a separate local variable.

functions.name: designated initializer for nested state
functions.description: Initialize only the meaningful fields of a complex aggregate.
functions.longDescription: Designated initializers improve readability in C by making field-to-value association explicit. They are especially valuable once structures get large, nested, or evolve over time.

functions.name: ownership naming convention
functions.description: Document ownership explicitly in a C API.
functions.longDescription: Document ownership explicitly in a C API.

functions.name: dynamic vector skeleton
functions.description: Grow a simple contiguous buffer while preserving existing values.
functions.longDescription: Grow a simple contiguous buffer while preserving existing values.

functions.name: linked list traversal
functions.description: Traverse a simple singly linked list.
functions.longDescription: A classic low-level iteration pattern for intrusive structures and embedded code.

functions.name: uthash lookup note
functions.description: Use uthash when a compact header-only C hash table is appropriate.
functions.longDescription: Use uthash when a compact header-only C hash table is appropriate.

functions.name: byte ring buffer push pop
functions.description: Maintain a fixed-capacity FIFO without dynamic allocation.
functions.longDescription: Maintain a fixed-capacity FIFO without dynamic allocation.

functions.name: bsearch lookup
functions.description: Binary search in a sorted C array.
functions.longDescription: bsearch is useful once the array is already sorted and repeated lookups matter.

functions.name: qsort comparator
functions.description: Sort a C array with qsort.
functions.longDescription: qsort is the standard C sorting helper. The comparator returns a negative, zero, or positive value.

functions.name: CSV simple split warning
functions.description: Parse a basic CSV-like line only when quoted fields are not required.
functions.longDescription: This lightweight approach is not a complete CSV parser: quoted commas, escapes, and embedded newlines require a dedicated parser.

functions.name: cJSON parse configuration
functions.description: Parse JSON with the third-party cJSON library.
functions.longDescription: Parse JSON with the third-party cJSON library.

functions.name: jansson load file
functions.description: Parse a JSON file with the third-party Jansson library.
functions.longDescription: Parse a JSON file with the third-party Jansson library.

functions.name: Linux inotify directory watch
functions.description: Watch filesystem changes through inotify on Linux.
functions.longDescription: Watch filesystem changes through inotify on Linux.

functions.name: POSIX directory traversal
functions.description: Enumerate files in a directory on POSIX.
functions.longDescription: A lightweight directory scan pattern for Linux and UNIX-like systems.

functions.name: POSIX flock advisory lock
functions.description: Protect a file against concurrent cooperating processes.
functions.longDescription: Protect a file against concurrent cooperating processes.

functions.name: POSIX open read close
functions.description: Use Linux/POSIX file descriptors for low-level I/O.
functions.longDescription: Use Linux/POSIX file descriptors for low-level I/O.

functions.name: fopen / fgets / fclose
functions.description: Read a text file line by line.
functions.longDescription: Basic pattern for text file reading in standard C.

functions.name: fseek ftell whole file read
functions.description: Read a complete seekable file into a NUL-terminated heap buffer.
functions.longDescription: Read a complete seekable file into a NUL-terminated heap buffer.

functions.name: fwrite / fread binary file
functions.description: Write and read binary data with stdio.
functions.longDescription: Useful for compact binary serialization or device-oriented data dumps.

functions.name: popen / _popen
functions.description: Run a command and capture its output.
functions.longDescription: Handy for diagnostics tools, command wrappers, and quick system integration.

functions.name: temporary file then atomic rename
functions.description: Write configuration through a temporary file, flush it, then rename it.
functions.longDescription: Write configuration through a temporary file, flush it, then rename it.

functions.name: big-endian frame encode/decode helpers
functions.description: Serialize integers explicitly instead of relying on host endianness or structure packing.
functions.longDescription: Binary protocols should define byte order explicitly. Reading and writing fields byte-by-byte is often safer than copying whole packed structs, especially across compilers, CPUs, and DLL boundaries.

functions.name: key=value parsing
functions.description: Parse a key=value configuration line.
functions.longDescription: Useful for lightweight INI-style parsing without additional dependencies.

functions.name: sscanf parse
functions.description: Parse structured text in C.
functions.longDescription: sscanf works well for compact fixed-format records.

functions.name: trim key value config line
functions.description: Parse a simple key=value line while ignoring blank lines and comments.
functions.longDescription: Parse a simple key=value line while ignoring blank lines and comments.

functions.name: Win32 file enumeration
functions.description: Enumerate files with FindFirstFile on Windows.
functions.longDescription: Native Windows file enumeration helper for simple utilities.

functions.name: Win32 file enumeration FindFirstFile
functions.description: Enumerate files matching a Windows wildcard.
functions.longDescription: Enumerate files matching a Windows wildcard.

functions.name: clock_gettime
functions.description: Measure elapsed time with POSIX clocks.
functions.longDescription: Monotonic clocks are safer than wall-clock time when measuring durations.

functions.name: cross-platform sleep
functions.description: Sleep for a short duration in portable C.
functions.longDescription: Simple pacing helper for polling loops and console tools.

functions.name: time strftime local time
functions.description: Format a wall-clock timestamp portably with platform-specific thread-safe localtime helpers.
functions.longDescription: Format a wall-clock timestamp portably with platform-specific thread-safe localtime helpers.

functions.name: Linux timerfd periodic timer
functions.description: Expose timer expirations as a Linux file descriptor suitable for poll or epoll.
functions.longDescription: Expose timer expirations as a Linux file descriptor suitable for poll or epoll.

functions.name: clock_gettime monotonic elapsed
functions.description: Measure elapsed time with a monotonic POSIX clock.
functions.longDescription: Measure elapsed time with a monotonic POSIX clock.

functions.name: nanosleep delay
functions.description: Sleep for a POSIX duration while handling interruption.
functions.longDescription: Sleep for a POSIX duration while handling interruption.

functions.name: deadline retry loop
functions.description: Retry an operation until success or a monotonic deadline expires.
functions.longDescription: Retry an operation until success or a monotonic deadline expires.

functions.name: timeout with retry policy
functions.description: Retry a fragile operation until success or deadline expiry.
functions.longDescription: Real hardware and OS services are often not ready on the first attempt. A bounded retry loop with a monotonic deadline is usually safer than a fixed number of blind retries because it makes the time budget explicit.

functions.name: Win32 high resolution performance counter
functions.description: Measure elapsed time with QueryPerformanceCounter on Windows.
functions.longDescription: Measure elapsed time with QueryPerformanceCounter on Windows.

functions.name: atomic compare exchange counter
functions.description: Update an atomic value conditionally.
functions.longDescription: Update an atomic value conditionally.

functions.name: C11 atomic flag
functions.description: Publish a simple flag atomically.
functions.longDescription: Publish a simple flag atomically.

functions.name: C11 mtx cnd producer consumer
functions.description: Coordinate a condition with a mutex using the C11 thread API.
functions.longDescription: Coordinate a condition with a mutex using the C11 thread API.

functions.name: C11 thrd_create join
functions.description: Create and join a standard C thread when the implementation provides threads.h.
functions.longDescription: Create and join a standard C thread when the implementation provides threads.h.

functions.name: ISR-safe byte ring buffer
functions.description: Exchange bytes between an interrupt handler and foreground code with fixed storage.
functions.longDescription: A fixed ring buffer is one of the simplest and most robust interrupt-to-foreground communication tools. In real firmware you must still reason about atomicity, IRQ masking, cache coherence, and single-producer/single-consumer assumptions.

functions.name: call_once C11
functions.description: Initialize shared state exactly once.
functions.longDescription: Initialize shared state exactly once.

functions.name: POSIX semaphore
functions.description: Synchronize workers with a POSIX semaphore.
functions.longDescription: Semaphores are useful when a producer must wake a consumer without transferring ownership of a mutex.

functions.name: pthread create / join
functions.description: Launch a worker thread with pthread.
functions.longDescription: The standard C threading API on POSIX systems. Add mutexes when shared state becomes more complex.

functions.name: pthread mutex + condition
functions.description: Wait for work with a POSIX condition variable.
functions.longDescription: Condition variables fit producer-consumer patterns and event-style synchronization.

functions.name: Win32 CreateThread
functions.description: Create a native worker thread on Windows.
functions.longDescription: Useful when integrating with Win32 APIs that already rely on HANDLE-based primitives.

functions.name: Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION
functions.description: Guard shared state with a Win32 critical section.
functions.longDescription: CRITICAL_SECTION is a lightweight intra-process lock commonly used in native Windows code.

functions.name: Win32 manual-reset event
functions.description: Signal work completion with a Win32 event.
functions.longDescription: Events are useful for one-shot notifications and thread coordination on Windows.

functions.name: Linux signalfd
functions.description: Receive selected Linux signals through a file descriptor.
functions.longDescription: Receive selected Linux signals through a file descriptor.

functions.name: Unix domain socket server skeleton
functions.description: Expose a local IPC endpoint through AF_UNIX on POSIX systems.
functions.longDescription: Expose a local IPC endpoint through AF_UNIX on POSIX systems.

functions.name: POSIX FIFO mkfifo
functions.description: Create a named FIFO on POSIX systems.
functions.longDescription: Named FIFOs are useful when two unrelated processes must exchange a byte stream without opening a socket.

functions.name: POSIX pipe() read/write
functions.description: Create an anonymous pipe on POSIX systems.
functions.longDescription: Anonymous pipes are useful for parent-child process communication and short-lived IPC.

functions.name: POSIX shared memory + mmap
functions.description: Create shared memory backed by shm_open.
functions.longDescription: Shared memory is useful for high-throughput IPC and real-time data exchange between local processes.

functions.name: CAN frame descriptor
functions.description: Represent a classical CAN frame in portable C.
functions.longDescription: A compact CAN frame struct is useful for protocol stacks, diagnostic tools, and test benches. Keep the payload length explicit and avoid depending on compiler-specific packing when the frame is only an in-memory representation.

functions.name: CRC16 incremental update
functions.description: Update a CRC16 over a byte stream in C.
functions.longDescription: Incremental CRC helpers are common in UART, CAN, RS-485, and file-transfer protocols because the checksum can be computed as bytes arrive instead of requiring one contiguous buffer.

functions.name: SPI transaction descriptor
functions.description: Describe one SPI exchange with explicit TX, RX, and chip-select behavior.
functions.longDescription: Many embedded drivers model SPI exchanges as descriptors so the high-level code can describe a transaction without depending on one specific HAL call shape.

functions.name: DMA-style circular descriptor buffer
functions.description: Manage producer and consumer positions in a fixed hardware-oriented ring.
functions.longDescription: DMA engines, UART drivers, and data acquisition firmware often rely on circular descriptor rings. The exact ownership rules vary by device, but the pattern is always the same: fixed storage, explicit producer/consumer indices, and clear full/empty conditions.

functions.name: register mask update with barrier
functions.description: Modify selected register bits without disturbing unrelated hardware fields.
functions.longDescription: A read-modify-write helper is common in embedded code because peripheral registers often pack several fields into one word. The barrier macro here is schematic, but the important idea is to preserve unrelated bits and respect the ordering rules required by the target bus and CPU.

functions.name: volatile register block
functions.description: Model hardware registers as a volatile structure at a fixed address.
functions.longDescription: For memory-mapped peripherals, volatile prevents the compiler from eliding or merging accesses that correspond to real hardware transactions. This pattern should still be paired with explicit masks, barriers, and hardware-specific sequencing where required.

functions.name: opaque handle API boundary
functions.description: Publish a stable C handle type while hiding the implementation details.
functions.longDescription: Forward-declaring the handle keeps the public C ABI small and stable while allowing the implementation to live in C or C++. This is one of the most common industrial interop techniques for DLLs, firmware services, and plugin boundaries.

functions.longDescription: Passing both sizeof(struct) and an explicit version number is a standard ABI-survival technique. It lets newer implementations detect what the caller understands and default missing trailing fields safely.

functions.name: CMake portable C executable
functions.signature:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(my_c_app VERSION 1.0 LANGUAGES C)

set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_C_EXTENSIONS OFF)

add_executable(my_c_app main.c sensor.c)
target_include_directories(my_c_app PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include)

if(MSVC)
    target_compile_options(my_c_app PRIVATE /W4)
else()
    target_compile_options(my_c_app PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
endif()

functions.name: Class skeleton
functions.description: Simple class skeleton.
functions.longDescription: Baseline class pattern with constructor, accessor, mutator, and private state.

functions.name: Abstract interface
functions.description: Pure virtual interface definition.
functions.longDescription: Useful for plugin systems, test doubles, hardware abstraction layers, and dependency injection.

functions.name: Single inheritance override
functions.description: Derived class overriding a virtual API.
functions.longDescription: Classic inheritance pattern with override for polymorphic behavior.

functions.name: Protected inheritance helper
functions.description: Protected helper members in a base class.
functions.longDescription: Shows how common implementation helpers can be exposed to derived classes while staying hidden from users.

functions.name: Composition over inheritance
functions.description: Inject a dependency as a member instead of inheriting.
functions.longDescription: Composition often keeps designs simpler and more testable than deep inheritance hierarchies.

functions.name: Java super() equivalent: base constructor initializer
functions.description: Initialize the base subobject from a derived constructor.
functions.longDescription: C++ has no super() keyword. Write Base(arguments) in the derived constructor initializer list.

functions.name: Java super.method() equivalent: Base::Method()
functions.description: Call a specific base implementation.
functions.longDescription: C++ uses scope resolution with the base-class name rather than super.method().

functions.name: Java instanceof equivalent: dynamic_cast pointer
functions.description: Check a runtime derived type through a polymorphic base pointer.
functions.longDescription: This is the closest direct equivalent of object instanceof Sensor. The base must be polymorphic, normally by declaring at least one virtual function.

functions.name: dynamic_cast reference throws std::bad_cast
functions.description: Checked downcast when a reference is required.
functions.longDescription: A failed pointer cast returns nullptr. A failed reference cast throws std::bad_cast.

functions.name: typeid exact runtime type check
functions.description: Compare the exact dynamic type.
functions.longDescription: typeid checks an exact type; dynamic_cast also matches derived classes.

functions.name: virtual dispatch instead of instanceof
functions.description: Prefer polymorphism when behavior varies by derived type.
functions.longDescription: Repeated instanceof-style branches often indicate that the operation belongs in a virtual member function.

functions.name: std::holds_alternative for std::variant
functions.description: Check an alternative in a closed type-safe union.
functions.longDescription: For a closed known type set, std::variant can be safer than RTTI.

functions.name: std::is_base_of_v compile-time relationship
functions.signature:
#include <type_traits>

static_assert(std::is_base_of_v<Device, Sensor>);
functions.description: Check inheritance at compile time.
functions.longDescription: This differs from instanceof: it asks a compile-time question about types, not a runtime object.
functions.declaration:
#include <type_traits>

static_assert(std::is_base_of_v<Device, Sensor>);

functions.name: Java-to-C++ inheritance quick reference
functions.description: Reference map for common Java spellings.
functions.longDescription: C++ expresses the same concepts with explicit base names, virtual functions, RTTI and scope resolution.

functions.name: operator<< stream output
functions.description: Custom stream insertion operator for debugging or logging.
functions.longDescription: A common operator overload for human-readable logging and debugging.

functions.name: operator+ value type
functions.description: Binary arithmetic operator on a value type.
functions.longDescription: Useful for math types or other lightweight value objects where operator semantics remain intuitive.

functions.name: operator[] indexing
functions.description: Subscript operator for an indexed class.
functions.longDescription: Provide both const and non-const overloads so indexed access works in read-only and writable contexts.

functions.name: defaulted comparisons
functions.description: Use modern comparison defaults when suitable.
functions.longDescription: Modern C++ can synthesize equality and ordering when memberwise comparison is acceptable.

functions.name: Container selection quick reference
functions.signature: Container selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::array<T, N>       fixed-size contiguous storage
 std::vector<T>         resizable contiguous storage; default sequence choice
 std::deque<T>          fast insertion/removal at both ends
 std::list<T>           stable iterators; O(1) splice/erase with iterator
 std::forward_list<T>   compact singly-linked list
 std::string            owning text buffer
 std::string_view       non-owning read-only text view
 std::span<T>           non-owning contiguous range view (C++20)
*/
functions.description: Choose a sequence container according to storage, iterator stability, and mutation needs.
functions.longDescription: Use std::vector by default for general sequences. Reach for list-like containers only when their specific iterator-stability or splice properties matter.
functions.declaration: Container selection quick reference

functions.name: std::array fixed-size container
functions.signature: std::array fixed-size container
functions.description: Store a fixed number of elements with container semantics.
functions.longDescription: std::array owns inline fixed-size storage and supports begin/end, size, at, front, back, and data.
functions.declaration: std::array fixed-size container

functions.name: std::array fill and data
functions.signature: std::array fill and data
functions.description: Fill fixed storage and expose a contiguous data pointer.
functions.longDescription: Useful for protocol buffers and hardware-facing APIs that expect pointer plus length.
functions.declaration: std::array fill and data

functions.name: std::vector basics
functions.signature: std::vector basics
functions.description: Use the default resizable contiguous sequence container.
functions.longDescription: std::vector is generally the first container to consider for dynamic sequences because it is cache-friendly and integrates well with STL algorithms.
functions.declaration: std::vector basics

functions.name: std::vector reserve capacity resize
functions.signature: std::vector reserve capacity resize
functions.description: Distinguish vector size from allocated capacity.
functions.longDescription: reserve avoids repeated reallocations when the approximate final number of elements is known. resize changes the logical number of elements.
functions.declaration: std::vector reserve capacity resize

functions.name: std::vector access at data front back
functions.signature: std::vector access at data front back
functions.description: Use the main vector element-access methods.
functions.longDescription: at throws std::out_of_range on invalid index. operator[] does not perform bounds checking.
functions.declaration: std::vector access at data front back

functions.name: std::vector insert erase range
functions.signature: std::vector insert erase range
functions.description: Insert or erase vector elements by iterator position or range.
functions.longDescription: Insertion and erasure can invalidate iterators and references because vector elements may move.
functions.declaration: std::vector insert erase range

functions.name: std::erase_if vector C++20
functions.signature: std::erase_if vector C++20
functions.description: Remove matching vector elements with the C++20 helper.
functions.longDescription: std::erase_if is the concise C++20 replacement for the classic erase-remove idiom on standard sequence containers.
functions.declaration: std::erase_if vector C++20

functions.name: std::deque push front and back
functions.signature: std::deque push front and back
functions.description: Use a double-ended queue with efficient operations at both ends.
functions.longDescription: std::deque is useful for sliding windows, work queues, and buffers that need push/pop at both front and back.
functions.declaration: std::deque push front and back

functions.name: std::list insert erase splice
functions.signature: std::list insert erase splice
functions.description: Use linked-list splice and iterator-safe erasure.
functions.longDescription: std::list provides stable iterators for unaffected elements and constant-time node splicing, but it has weaker cache locality than vector.
functions.declaration: std::list insert erase splice

functions.name: std::list sort unique remove_if
functions.signature: std::list sort unique remove_if
functions.description: Use list-specific algorithms that relink nodes.
functions.longDescription: std::list exposes member algorithms because generic algorithms such as std::sort require random-access iterators.
functions.declaration: std::list sort unique remove_if

functions.name: std::forward_list insert_after erase_after
functions.signature: std::forward_list insert_after erase_after
functions.description: Manipulate a singly-linked list through before-position iterators.
functions.longDescription: std::forward_list minimizes node overhead but only supports forward traversal and after-position insertion/erasure.
functions.declaration: std::forward_list insert_after erase_after

functions.name: std::string common methods
functions.signature: std::string common methods
functions.description: Use common owning string operations.
functions.longDescription: starts_with and ends_with are available in C++20. std::string manages its own writable contiguous character storage.
functions.declaration: std::string common methods

functions.name: std::string_view non-owning view
functions.signature: std::string_view non-owning view
functions.description: Pass read-only text without allocating or copying.
functions.longDescription: std::string_view does not own its characters. Ensure the referenced buffer outlives the view.
functions.declaration: std::string_view non-owning view

functions.name: std::span contiguous view C++20
functions.signature: std::span contiguous view C++20
functions.description: Expose a non-owning view over contiguous elements.
functions.longDescription: std::span works with arrays, vectors, and pointer-size pairs while preserving size information.
functions.declaration: std::span contiguous view C++20

functions.name: std::bitset fixed bit flags
functions.signature: std::bitset fixed bit flags
functions.description: Store a compile-time fixed number of bits.
functions.longDescription: std::bitset is not a general STL container, but it is useful for fixed flag fields, masks, and diagnostics.
functions.declaration: std::bitset fixed bit flags

functions.name: Ordered associative selection quick reference
functions.signature: Ordered associative selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::set<Key>              unique sorted keys
 std::multiset<Key>         sorted keys with duplicates
 std::map<Key, Value>       unique sorted key/value pairs
 std::multimap<Key, Value>  sorted key/value pairs with duplicate keys

 Typical lookup cost: logarithmic.
 Use when sorted iteration, lower_bound, or range queries matter.
*/
functions.description: Choose ordered tree-based associative containers.
functions.longDescription: Ordered associative containers maintain key ordering and expose lower_bound, upper_bound, and equal_range.
functions.declaration: Ordered associative selection quick reference

functions.name: std::set insert contains find erase
functions.signature: std::set insert contains find erase
functions.description: Manage unique sorted keys.
functions.longDescription: std::set stores each key once and keeps iteration ordered according to its comparator.
functions.declaration: std::set insert contains find erase

functions.name: std::multiset duplicates equal_range
functions.signature: std::multiset duplicates equal_range
functions.description: Store sorted duplicate keys and traverse one equivalent-key range.
functions.longDescription: std::multiset preserves all duplicates. count and equal_range are useful when grouping equal values.
functions.declaration: std::multiset duplicates equal_range

functions.name: std::map insert access try_emplace
functions.signature: std::map insert access try_emplace
functions.description: Store unique sorted key/value pairs and use modern insertion helpers.
functions.longDescription: operator[] may default-construct a mapped value. try_emplace avoids constructing the mapped value when the key already exists.
functions.declaration: std::map insert access try_emplace

functions.name: std::map find contains at erase
functions.signature: std::map find contains at erase
functions.description: Perform safe ordered-map lookup and erasure.
functions.longDescription: find does not insert. at provides checked access and throws when the key is missing.
functions.declaration: std::map find contains at erase

functions.name: std::multimap duplicate keys
functions.signature: std::multimap duplicate keys
functions.description: Store multiple mapped values for one ordered key.
functions.longDescription: Use std::multimap when duplicate keys are meaningful and ordered grouping is desirable.
functions.declaration: std::multimap duplicate keys

functions.name: lower_bound upper_bound equal_range
functions.signature: lower_bound upper_bound equal_range
functions.description: Perform ordered boundary and range searches.
functions.longDescription: Boundary-search methods are a major reason to choose an ordered associative container over an unordered hash table.
functions.declaration: lower_bound upper_bound equal_range

functions.name: std::map custom comparator
functions.signature: std::map custom comparator
functions.description: Provide an ordering policy for ordered containers.
functions.longDescription: A comparator defines key equivalence and ordering. It must satisfy strict weak ordering.
functions.declaration: std::map custom comparator

functions.name: Unordered associative selection quick reference
functions.signature: Unordered associative selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::unordered_set<Key>              unique hashed keys
 std::unordered_multiset<Key>         hashed keys with duplicates
 std::unordered_map<Key, Value>       unique hashed key/value pairs
 std::unordered_multimap<Key, Value>  hashed key/value pairs with duplicate keys

 Average lookup cost is typically constant-time.
 Iteration order is unspecified and can change after rehash.
*/
functions.description: Choose hash-table associative containers.
functions.longDescription: Use unordered containers for key-based lookup when sorted iteration and ordered range queries are not required.
functions.declaration: Unordered associative selection quick reference

functions.name: std::unordered_set insert contains erase
functions.signature: std::unordered_set insert contains erase
functions.description: Manage unique hashed keys.
functions.longDescription: unordered_set provides efficient average-case key lookup without sorted traversal.
functions.declaration: std::unordered_set insert contains erase

functions.name: std::unordered_multiset duplicates
functions.signature: std::unordered_multiset duplicates
functions.description: Store duplicate hashed keys.
functions.longDescription: unordered_multiset groups equivalent values into ranges but does not guarantee global iteration order.
functions.declaration: std::unordered_multiset duplicates

functions.name: std::unordered_map basics
functions.signature: std::unordered_map basics
functions.description: Store unique hashed key/value pairs.
functions.longDescription: unordered_map is a common choice for fast dictionary-style lookup when key ordering is irrelevant.
functions.declaration: std::unordered_map basics

functions.name: unordered_map find contains at erase
functions.signature: unordered_map find contains at erase
functions.description: Use non-inserting lookup and erasure on a hash map.
functions.longDescription: Prefer find, contains, or at when a missing key should not silently create a new mapped value.
functions.declaration: unordered_map find contains at erase

functions.name: std::unordered_multimap duplicate keys
functions.signature: std::unordered_multimap duplicate keys
functions.description: Store several hashed values for one key.
functions.longDescription: Use equal_range to process all values associated with a duplicate key.
functions.declaration: std::unordered_multimap duplicate keys

functions.name: reserve rehash load_factor
functions.signature: reserve rehash load_factor
functions.description: Tune hash-table capacity and inspect its load.
functions.longDescription: reserve can reduce rehash operations when the approximate number of elements is known. Rehash invalidates iterators.
functions.declaration: reserve rehash load_factor

functions.name: custom hash for struct key
functions.signature: custom hash for struct key
functions.description: Provide equality and hashing for a custom key type.
functions.longDescription: A hash function should be stable for the lifetime of a key stored in the table and should distribute values reasonably.
functions.declaration: custom hash for struct key

functions.name: unordered iteration and invalidation reminder
functions.signature: unordered iteration and invalidation reminder
functions.insertText:
/*
 unordered_* iteration order is unspecified.
 Rehashing invalidates iterators.
 References and pointers to stored elements remain valid across rehash,
 but erasing an element invalidates references and pointers to that element.
 Do not rely on output ordering for logs or serialized files.
*/
functions.description: Document the main unordered-container pitfalls.
functions.longDescription: Use an ordered container or sort extracted keys when deterministic iteration order is required.
functions.declaration: unordered iteration and invalidation reminder

functions.name: Container adaptor selection quick reference
functions.signature: Container adaptor selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::stack<T>          LIFO access: push, pop, top
 std::queue<T>          FIFO access: push, pop, front, back
 std::priority_queue<T> highest-priority access: push, pop, top

 Adaptors deliberately expose a restricted interface.
*/
functions.description: Choose a restricted queue-like interface when full container traversal is unnecessary.
functions.longDescription: Container adaptors express access policy clearly and prevent accidental arbitrary mutation.
functions.declaration: Container adaptor selection quick reference

functions.name: std::stack LIFO
functions.signature: std::stack LIFO
functions.description: Use last-in-first-out storage.
functions.longDescription: std::stack is suitable for parsing, undo state, depth-first traversal, and explicit work stacks.
functions.declaration: std::stack LIFO

functions.name: std::queue FIFO
functions.signature: std::queue FIFO
functions.description: Use first-in-first-out storage.
functions.longDescription: std::queue is suitable for event processing and breadth-first work scheduling.
functions.declaration: std::queue FIFO

functions.name: std::priority_queue max heap
functions.signature: std::priority_queue max heap
functions.description: Use a max-priority queue.
functions.longDescription: std::priority_queue exposes the highest-priority element at top by default.
functions.declaration: std::priority_queue max heap

functions.name: std::priority_queue min heap comparator
functions.signature: std::priority_queue min heap comparator
functions.description: Invert the comparator to create a min-priority queue.
functions.longDescription: The underlying sequence defaults to std::vector. The comparator defines which element appears at top.
functions.declaration: std::priority_queue min heap comparator

functions.name: Iterator category quick reference
functions.signature: Iterator category quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 input iterator         read a single-pass sequence
 output iterator        write a single-pass sequence
 forward iterator       multi-pass forward traversal
 bidirectional iterator forward and backward traversal
 random-access iterator constant-time jumps and ordering
 contiguous iterator    random-access plus contiguous memory (C++20)
*/
functions.description: Understand iterator capability levels.
functions.longDescription: Algorithms declare the minimum iterator capabilities they require. For example, std::sort needs random-access iterators, while std::find only needs input iterators.
functions.declaration: Iterator category quick reference

functions.name: container iterator types
functions.signature: container iterator types
functions.description: Use mutable, const, reverse, and const-reverse iterators.
functions.longDescription: Prefer auto for local iterator declarations unless the explicit type improves teaching or API clarity.
functions.declaration: container iterator types

functions.name: classic indexed loop
functions.signature: classic indexed loop
functions.description: Traverse an indexable sequence while retaining its index.
functions.longDescription: Use an indexed loop when the numeric position is required. Prefer range-for for simple value traversal.
functions.declaration: classic indexed loop

functions.name: classic iterator loop
functions.signature: classic iterator loop
functions.description: Traverse a container explicitly through iterators.
functions.longDescription: Explicit iterators are useful when passing positions to insert/erase or when controlling traversal precisely.
functions.declaration: classic iterator loop

functions.name: const iterator loop
functions.signature: const iterator loop
functions.description: Traverse without permitting element mutation.
functions.longDescription: cbegin and cend make read-only intent explicit.
functions.declaration: const iterator loop

functions.name: range-based for value reference const reference
functions.signature: range-based for value reference const reference
functions.description: Choose copy, mutable reference, or const reference in a range-for loop.
functions.longDescription: For non-trivial elements, const T& is the usual read-only traversal form. Use T& when mutating elements.
functions.declaration: range-based for value reference const reference

functions.name: structured bindings map traversal
functions.signature: structured bindings map traversal
functions.description: Traverse associative key/value pairs with structured bindings.
functions.longDescription: Use auto& or const auto& to avoid copying each pair.
functions.declaration: structured bindings map traversal

functions.name: erase while iterating sequence
functions.signature: erase while iterating sequence
functions.description: Erase elements safely while traversing a sequence container.
functions.longDescription: erase returns the next valid iterator. Do not increment twice after an erase.
functions.declaration: erase while iterating sequence

functions.name: erase while iterating map
functions.signature: erase while iterating map
functions.description: Erase associative entries safely during traversal.
functions.longDescription: Modern associative-container erase(iterator) returns the iterator following the removed element.
functions.declaration: erase while iterating map

functions.name: reverse iterator loop
functions.signature: reverse iterator loop
functions.description: Traverse a bidirectional or random-access sequence in reverse order.
functions.longDescription: rbegin and rend return reverse iterators and work with containers that support reverse traversal.
functions.declaration: reverse iterator loop

functions.name: std::next prev advance distance
functions.signature: std::next prev advance distance
functions.description: Move iterators generically and measure a range distance.
functions.longDescription: Complexity depends on iterator category: constant-time for random-access iterators, linear for list iterators.
functions.declaration: std::next prev advance distance

functions.name: std::begin end size data generic helpers
functions.signature: std::begin end size data generic helpers
functions.description: Use generic range helpers with arrays and containers.
functions.longDescription: std::begin and std::end enable generic traversal of built-in arrays as well as standard containers.
functions.declaration: std::begin end size data generic helpers

functions.name: back_inserter front_inserter inserter
functions.signature: back_inserter front_inserter inserter
functions.description: Adapt insertion operations into output iterators.
functions.longDescription: Insertion iterators are especially useful as destinations for copy, transform, and ranges algorithms.
functions.declaration: back_inserter front_inserter inserter

functions.name: istream_iterator ostream_iterator
functions.signature: istream_iterator ostream_iterator
functions.description: Read or write sequences through stream iterators.
functions.longDescription: Stream iterators bridge formatted I/O and STL algorithms.
functions.declaration: istream_iterator ostream_iterator

functions.name: ranges filter transform pipeline C++20
functions.signature: ranges filter transform pipeline C++20
functions.description: Compose lazy traversal views with C++20 ranges.
functions.longDescription: Views generally do not own the underlying elements. Ensure the source range outlives the view pipeline.
functions.declaration: ranges filter transform pipeline C++20

functions.name: ranges subrange iterator pair
functions.signature: ranges subrange iterator pair
functions.description: Wrap an iterator/sentinel pair as a range.
functions.longDescription: std::ranges::subrange is useful for exposing slices without copying elements.
functions.declaration: ranges subrange iterator pair

functions.name: Range-based for
functions.description: Iterate over a container with range-for.
functions.longDescription: The most readable pattern for simple sequential traversal of a container.

functions.name: Iterator loop
functions.description: Explicit iterator traversal.
functions.longDescription: Useful when you need iterator arithmetic, insertion positions, or compatibility with older code.

functions.name: Map traversal with structured bindings
functions.description: Walk through a map using structured bindings.
functions.longDescription: Structured bindings make associative container traversal concise and readable.

functions.name: Algorithm selection quick reference
functions.signature: Algorithm selection quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 Search:       find, find_if, count_if, any_of, all_of, none_of
 Ordering:     sort, stable_sort, partial_sort, nth_element, lower_bound
 Mutation:     remove_if + erase, std::erase_if, unique, partition
 Transform:    transform, for_each, copy, move
 Aggregation:  accumulate, reduce, minmax_element
 Sets:         set_union, set_intersection, set_difference
 C++20 ranges: std::ranges::find, sort, transform, views pipelines
*/
functions.description: Prefer standard algorithms when they state the intent clearly.
functions.longDescription: Algorithms work through iterators or ranges and reduce manual loop boilerplate.
functions.declaration: Algorithm selection quick reference

functions.name: std::find and std::find_if
functions.signature: std::find and std::find_if
functions.description: Search by equality or predicate.
functions.longDescription: find_if is a general-purpose search tool for containers that do not provide a more specialized member lookup.
functions.declaration: std::find and std::find_if

functions.name: count_if any_of all_of none_of
functions.signature: count_if any_of all_of none_of
functions.description: Evaluate predicates across a range.
functions.longDescription: These algorithms express intent more directly than manual boolean accumulator loops.
functions.declaration: count_if any_of all_of none_of

functions.name: std::sort stable_sort comparator
functions.signature: std::sort stable_sort comparator
functions.description: Sort random-access ranges with optional stability.
functions.longDescription: stable_sort preserves the relative order of equivalent elements. std::list instead uses its member sort.
functions.declaration: std::sort stable_sort comparator

functions.name: partial_sort nth_element
functions.signature: partial_sort nth_element
functions.description: Compute a sorted prefix or partition around one rank.
functions.longDescription: Use these algorithms when a fully sorted range would do unnecessary work.
functions.declaration: partial_sort nth_element

functions.name: lower_bound binary_search sorted vector
functions.signature: lower_bound binary_search sorted vector
functions.description: Search efficiently inside an already sorted random-access sequence.
functions.longDescription: A sorted vector can be a strong read-heavy alternative to tree containers due to compact storage and cache locality.
functions.declaration: lower_bound binary_search sorted vector

functions.name: erase-remove idiom pre C++20
functions.signature: erase-remove idiom pre C++20
functions.description: Remove matching sequence elements before C++20.
functions.longDescription: remove_if compacts retained elements and returns the logical new end; erase then removes the trailing range physically.
functions.declaration: erase-remove idiom pre C++20

functions.name: sort unique erase deduplicate vector
functions.signature: sort unique erase deduplicate vector
functions.description: Deduplicate a vector by sorting then erasing adjacent duplicates.
functions.longDescription: Use a set instead when uniqueness must be enforced incrementally and ordering semantics are acceptable.
functions.declaration: sort unique erase deduplicate vector

functions.name: partition stable_partition
functions.signature: partition stable_partition
functions.description: Split a range according to a predicate.
functions.longDescription: stable_partition preserves relative order within each partition but may perform more work.
functions.declaration: partition stable_partition

functions.name: std::transform with back_inserter
functions.signature: std::transform with back_inserter
functions.description: Map a range into another container.
functions.longDescription: Transform states element-wise conversion intent clearly and works with insertion iterators.
functions.declaration: std::transform with back_inserter

functions.name: std::for_each and std::ranges::for_each
functions.signature: std::for_each and std::ranges::for_each
functions.description: Apply an operation to every element.
functions.longDescription: A range-for loop can be equally readable for simple cases. Algorithms compose better in generic code.
functions.declaration: std::for_each and std::ranges::for_each

functions.name: std::accumulate
functions.signature: std::accumulate
functions.description: Fold a range sequentially into one result.
functions.longDescription: The initial value determines the accumulation type, so use 0.0 rather than 0 for floating-point sums.
functions.declaration: std::accumulate

functions.name: std::minmax_element
functions.signature: std::minmax_element
functions.description: Find minimum and maximum elements in one pass.
functions.longDescription: Always check the empty-range case before dereferencing returned iterators.
functions.declaration: std::minmax_element

functions.name: std::copy copy_if move
functions.signature: std::copy copy_if move
functions.description: Copy filtered values or move resources into another range.
functions.longDescription: std::move algorithm moves each element; moved-from objects remain valid but their values are generally unspecified.
functions.declaration: std::copy copy_if move

functions.name: set_union intersection difference
functions.signature: set_union intersection difference
functions.description: Apply set operations to sorted ranges.
functions.longDescription: Inputs must be sorted according to the same ordering. Related algorithms include set_union, set_difference, and includes.
functions.declaration: set_union intersection difference

functions.name: std::ranges find sort projections C++20
functions.signature: std::ranges find sort projections C++20
functions.description: Use C++20 ranges algorithms and projections.
functions.longDescription: Projections can eliminate small adapter lambdas when sorting or searching by a member or accessor.
functions.declaration: std::ranges find sort projections C++20

functions.name: std::find_if
functions.description: Search a container with a predicate.
functions.longDescription: Part of the standard algorithm library and often preferable to manual loops.

functions.name: erase-remove idiom
functions.description: Remove matching values from a sequence container.
functions.longDescription: Classic pattern for removing elements from std::vector and similar sequence containers.

functions.name: std::transform
functions.description: Transform one container into another.
functions.longDescription: Use standard algorithms to express intent clearly and avoid index-heavy loops.

functions.name: lambda syntax quick reference
functions.signature: lambda syntax quick reference
functions.insertText:
/*
 []                 capture nothing
 [value]            capture one variable by value
 [&value]           capture one variable by reference
 [=]                capture used automatic variables by value
 [&]                capture used automatic variables by reference
 [&, threshold]     reference default, threshold by value
 [=, &result]       value default, result by reference
 [this]             capture this pointer
 [*this]            copy current object (C++17)
 [name = expression] init-capture (C++14)
*/
functions.description: Reference the main lambda capture forms.
functions.longDescription: Capture deliberately. Avoid broad reference captures for callbacks that can outlive the current scope.
functions.declaration: lambda syntax quick reference

functions.name: captureless lambda
functions.signature: captureless lambda
functions.description: Declare a lambda without external state.
functions.longDescription: Captureless lambdas are small function objects and can convert to compatible plain function pointers.
functions.declaration: captureless lambda

functions.name: capture by value and reference
functions.signature: capture by value and reference
functions.description: Mix value and reference captures explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Value captures copy state into the closure. Reference captures alias external state and require careful lifetime management.
functions.declaration: capture by value and reference

functions.name: default captures and explicit exceptions
functions.signature: default captures and explicit exceptions
functions.description: Use a default capture while overriding one variable explicitly.
functions.longDescription: Prefer explicit captures in long-lived callbacks because they make dependencies and lifetimes easier to audit.
functions.declaration: default captures and explicit exceptions

functions.name: capture this and copy object
functions.signature: capture this and copy object
functions.description: Distinguish capturing an object pointer from copying the object state.
functions.longDescription: [this] captures a pointer and can dangle. [*this] copies the current object into the closure when the type is copyable.
functions.declaration: capture this and copy object

functions.name: init capture and move capture
functions.signature: init capture and move capture
functions.description: Initialize a closure field directly and move ownership into a callback.
functions.longDescription: Init-capture is useful for renamed variables, computed capture values, and move-only resources.
functions.declaration: init capture and move capture

functions.name: mutable lambda state
functions.signature: mutable lambda state
functions.description: Permit mutation of values stored inside the closure object.
functions.longDescription: By-value captures are logically const inside operator() unless mutable is specified.
functions.declaration: mutable lambda state

functions.name: Lambda capture by value/reference
functions.description: Basic lambda capture patterns.
functions.longDescription: Captures by value copy external state, while captures by reference let the lambda observe or modify external variables.

functions.name: Generic lambda
functions.description: Lambda with auto parameters.
functions.longDescription: Generic lambdas provide a concise way to write templated function objects.

functions.name: lambda parameters return type noexcept
functions.signature: lambda parameters return type noexcept
functions.description: Specify parameters, exception contract, and trailing return type.
functions.longDescription: A trailing return type is useful when deduction is insufficient or when documenting an interface.
functions.declaration: lambda parameters return type noexcept

functions.name: generic lambda auto parameters
functions.signature: generic lambda auto parameters
functions.description: Declare a templated call operator through auto parameters.
functions.longDescription: Generic lambdas are concise reusable function objects for algorithms and local helpers.
functions.declaration: generic lambda auto parameters

functions.name: templated lambda C++20
functions.signature: templated lambda C++20
functions.description: Declare explicit template parameters on a lambda.
functions.longDescription: C++20 templated lambdas are useful when auto parameters alone cannot express the intended constraints or explicit template arguments.
functions.declaration: templated lambda C++20

functions.name: constexpr lambda
functions.signature: constexpr lambda
functions.description: Use a lambda during constant evaluation when its body permits it.
functions.longDescription: Lambdas can participate in compile-time computation and local metaprogramming helpers.
functions.declaration: constexpr lambda

functions.name: recursive lambda self parameter
functions.signature: recursive lambda self parameter
functions.description: Implement a local recursive lambda without std::function.
functions.longDescription: Passing the closure to itself avoids type-erasure overhead and keeps the helper local.
functions.declaration: recursive lambda self parameter

functions.name: lambda immediately invoked
functions.signature: lambda immediately invoked
functions.description: Use an immediately invoked lambda to compute a const value with structured control flow.
functions.longDescription: This pattern keeps temporary setup logic local while allowing const initialization.
functions.declaration: lambda immediately invoked

functions.name: captureless lambda to function pointer
functions.signature: captureless lambda to function pointer
functions.description: Convert a captureless lambda to a compatible function pointer.
functions.longDescription: A lambda with captures cannot convert to a plain function pointer because it needs closure state.
functions.declaration: captureless lambda to function pointer

functions.name: std::function callback storage
functions.signature: std::function callback storage
functions.description: Store heterogeneous copyable callbacks behind one signature.
functions.longDescription: std::function performs type erasure and may allocate. Use it when runtime callback replacement is useful and the overhead is acceptable.
functions.declaration: std::function callback storage

functions.name: template callback parameter zero overhead
functions.signature: template callback parameter zero overhead
functions.description: Accept an arbitrary callback without std::function type erasure.
functions.longDescription: A forwarding template is suitable for hot paths and compile-time composition when ABI stability is not required.
functions.declaration: template callback parameter zero overhead

functions.name: std::invoke function member pointer callable
functions.signature: std::invoke function member pointer callable
functions.description: Invoke functions, function objects, member functions, or data-member pointers uniformly.
functions.longDescription: std::invoke is a useful generic dispatch primitive for callback frameworks and adapter utilities.
functions.declaration: std::invoke function member pointer callable

functions.name: std::bind and lambda preferred
functions.signature: std::bind and lambda preferred
functions.description: Bind arguments to a callable and compare with the clearer lambda form.
functions.longDescription: std::bind remains available, but lambdas usually make captures, argument order, and lifetimes easier to read.
functions.declaration: std::bind and lambda preferred

functions.name: callback list event dispatch
functions.signature: callback list event dispatch
functions.description: Store and emit a simple list of callbacks.
functions.longDescription: For production event systems, add disconnection tokens, exception policy, reentrancy rules, and thread-safety decisions.
functions.declaration: callback list event dispatch

functions.name: std::function callback
functions.description: Store a callback using std::function.
functions.longDescription: Good for callbacks with type erasure, though function pointers or templates may be cheaper in hot paths.

functions.name: avoid dangling reference capture
functions.signature: avoid dangling reference capture
functions.description: Recognize the lifetime hazard of returning a reference-capturing callback.
functions.longDescription: Reference captures are safe only while the referenced objects remain alive. This is especially important for deferred, GUI, and threaded callbacks.
functions.declaration: avoid dangling reference capture

functions.name: weak_ptr callback guard
functions.signature: weak_ptr callback guard
functions.description: Avoid extending lifetime or dereferencing a destroyed shared object from a callback.
functions.longDescription: weak_ptr is a common technique for callbacks registered into systems that may outlive the observed object.
functions.declaration: weak_ptr callback guard

functions.name: thread lambda value capture
functions.signature: thread lambda value capture
functions.description: Move owned state into a worker thread callback.
functions.longDescription: Avoid capturing stack references unless the thread is joined before those references can expire and concurrent access is synchronized.
functions.declaration: thread lambda value capture

functions.name: jthread stop token callback C++20
functions.signature: jthread stop token callback C++20
functions.description: Use a cooperative cancellation token inside a C++20 jthread callback.
functions.longDescription: std::jthread requests stop and joins automatically at destruction, which is safer than unmanaged thread shutdown paths.
functions.declaration: jthread stop token callback C++20

functions.name: move-only callback note C++23
functions.signature: move-only callback note C++23
functions.insertText:
/*
 C++23: std::move_only_function<void()> can store move-only callables.
 Use it when a callback owns move-only state and copying is not required.
 Availability depends on the selected standard library implementation.
*/
functions.description: Document the C++23 move-only callback wrapper.
functions.longDescription: std::function requires copyable targets. std::move_only_function addresses callbacks that intentionally own move-only resources.
functions.declaration: move-only callback note C++23

functions.name: Write file with ofstream
functions.description: Write formatted text to a file.
functions.longDescription: Simple file output example using the standard stream API.
