functions.description: Read raw bytes from a binary file.
functions.longDescription: Convenient for loading firmware images, packed resources, or protocol captures into memory.

functions.name: stringstream parsing
functions.description: Parse tokens from an in-memory string.
functions.longDescription: Useful for simple text parsing when the data is already present in memory.

functions.name: read entire text file
functions.description: Read a complete text file into one std::string.
functions.longDescription: Use this when the file is small enough to hold in memory, for example configuration files, templates, JSON payloads, or text resources.

functions.name: std::filesystem path and recursive iteration
functions.description: Manipulate paths and enumerate files with the standard filesystem library.
functions.longDescription: std::filesystem is the standard C++17 API for paths, directory traversal, metadata queries, copy, rename, and removal operations.

functions.name: std::filesystem copy rename remove
functions.description: Copy, rename, and remove files with std::filesystem.
functions.longDescription: Prefer std::filesystem over ad hoc platform commands when working with regular files and directories.

functions.name: atomic-ish config replace
functions.description: Write a temporary configuration file and replace the previous file only after the write succeeds.
functions.longDescription: Writing to a temporary file before replacement reduces the risk of leaving a partially-written configuration file. Exact replacement semantics differ between operating systems, so production code may need a platform-specific final replace step.

functions.name: stream state validation
functions.description: Check stream status after parsing or writing.
functions.longDescription: Stream extraction operations communicate malformed or missing data through the stream state. Check the state instead of assuming parsing succeeded.

functions.name: nlohmann json dependency note
functions.description: Document the common JSON for Modern C++ dependency.
functions.longDescription: nlohmann/json is a third-party C++ library. It is not part of the C++ standard library. It provides a convenient JSON value type, parsing helpers, serialization helpers, and conversions.

functions.name: parse JSON file with nlohmann json
functions.description: Parse a JSON configuration file through std::ifstream.
functions.longDescription: The JSON parser can consume an input stream directly. Catch nlohmann::json::parse_error when malformed user configuration must be reported cleanly.

functions.name: safe JSON field access
functions.description: Read required and optional JSON fields deliberately.
functions.longDescription: Use at() for required fields, value() for optional fields with defaults, and contains() when the presence of a member changes behavior.

functions.name: write JSON file pretty print
functions.description: Serialize a JSON object to disk with indentation.
functions.longDescription: dump(4) produces human-readable indentation. Use compact dump() without an indentation argument for smaller payloads sent over a network.

functions.name: JSON parse error handling
functions.description: Report malformed JSON without crashing the application.
functions.longDescription: The parse_error exception includes diagnostic text and a byte position. This is useful for configuration editors and operator-facing tools.

functions.name: JSON parse without exceptions
functions.description: Parse JSON while returning a discarded value on failure.
functions.longDescription: Disable exceptions only when a simple success/failure result is sufficient. In this mode, detailed parse diagnostics are not available.

functions.name: custom type to_json from_json
functions.description: Serialize and deserialize a domain structure with nlohmann/json.
functions.longDescription: Free to_json and from_json functions make domain structures easier to exchange with JavaScript frontends and configuration files.

functions.name: minimal key value config parser
functions.description: Parse a simple key=value file without an external dependency.
functions.longDescription: A small parser is sufficient for controlled internal files. For comments, escaping, sections, Unicode, validation, or round-trip editing, use a dedicated configuration library.

functions.name: Boost PropertyTree read INI
functions.description: Read an INI file with Boost.PropertyTree.
functions.longDescription: Boost.PropertyTree provides read_ini and write_ini helpers. It is useful when a project already depends on Boost and a lightweight hierarchical configuration tree is sufficient.

functions.name: toml++ parse file
functions.description: Parse a typed TOML configuration file with toml++.
functions.longDescription: toml++ is a third-party parser for TOML configuration files. TOML is useful when humans frequently edit settings and typed values matter.

functions.name: environment variable override
functions.description: Override a configuration field from an environment variable.
functions.longDescription: Environment variables are useful for deployment overrides, CI jobs, and secrets that should not be committed to a configuration file.

functions.name: simple CSV line parser
functions.description: Split a simple comma-separated line.
functions.longDescription: This minimal parser does not handle quoted commas, escaped quotes, or multiline fields. Use a dedicated CSV library for general CSV input.

functions.name: write delimited measurements
functions.description: Write a compact CSV-like measurement log.
functions.longDescription: Delimited text remains practical for test benches, spreadsheets, and quick data analysis. Define quoting and decimal conventions explicitly if fields can contain text.

functions.name: binary vector write
functions.description: Write raw byte storage to a binary file.
functions.longDescription: Raw binary files are useful for captures and firmware images. Define endianness, versioning, and record boundaries when the format must remain stable.

functions.name: binary POD warning
functions.signature:
/*
 Avoid writing an arbitrary C++ object with:
 output.write(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(&object), sizeof(object));

 Padding, endianness, pointer members, virtual tables, ABI changes,
 compiler differences, and version changes can make the file unstable.
 Serialize fields explicitly or use a serialization format/library.
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Avoid writing an arbitrary C++ object with:
 output.write(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(&object), sizeof(object));

 Padding, endianness, pointer members, virtual tables, ABI changes,
 compiler differences, and version changes can make the file unstable.
 Serialize fields explicitly or use a serialization format/library.
*/
functions.description: Document why dumping arbitrary C++ objects is unsafe.
functions.longDescription: Direct memory dumps are only appropriate for tightly-controlled trivial layouts with an explicit binary contract.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Avoid writing an arbitrary C++ object with:
 output.write(reinterpret_cast<const char *>(&object), sizeof(object));

 Padding, endianness, pointer members, virtual tables, ABI changes,
 compiler differences, and version changes can make the file unstable.
 Serialize fields explicitly or use a serialization format/library.
*/

functions.name: POSIX open read close file descriptor
functions.description: Open and read a file with Linux/POSIX file descriptors.
functions.longDescription: Use the POSIX file-descriptor API when integrating with Linux-specific devices, descriptors, poll/epoll, or system calls. For ordinary portable files, prefer std::ifstream and std::filesystem.

functions.name: POSIX write all bytes helper
functions.description: Write an entire byte buffer while handling partial writes and EINTR.
functions.longDescription: POSIX write may transfer fewer bytes than requested. Loop until the complete buffer has been written, and retry interrupted calls.

functions.name: Linux mmap read-only file mapping
functions.description: Map a file into memory for efficient random access on Linux.
functions.longDescription: Memory mapping is useful for large binary files and random access. The mapped region remains valid until munmap. Handle zero-length files separately in production code.

functions.name: Linux inotify watch directory
functions.description: Watch a Linux directory for file changes with inotify.
functions.longDescription: inotify is Linux-specific. It is appropriate for configuration hot-reload, file arrival monitoring, and local tooling. Integrate the descriptor with poll or epoll for non-blocking event loops.

functions.name: POSIX mkstemp temporary file
functions.description: Create a unique temporary file securely with mkstemp.
functions.longDescription: mkstemp creates and opens a unique file atomically, avoiding race conditions from manually generated temporary paths.

functions.name: Linux flock advisory file lock
functions.description: Protect a process-shared file with an advisory flock lock.
functions.longDescription: flock provides advisory locking on Linux. All cooperating processes must follow the same locking convention.

functions.name: serial and USB API selection quick reference
functions.signature:
/*
 Serial / UART over COM or tty:
   - portable C++ wrapper: Boost.Asio serial_port
   - Windows native API: CreateFile + DCB + ReadFile/WriteFile
   - POSIX native API: open + termios + read/write

 USB device access:
   - generic USB transfers: libusb
   - USB HID or Bluetooth HID: HIDAPI
   - vendor SDK / OS driver API when required by the device
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Serial / UART over COM or tty:
   - portable C++ wrapper: Boost.Asio serial_port
   - Windows native API: CreateFile + DCB + ReadFile/WriteFile
   - POSIX native API: open + termios + read/write

 USB device access:
   - generic USB transfers: libusb
   - USB HID or Bluetooth HID: HIDAPI
   - vendor SDK / OS driver API when required by the device
*/
functions.description: Choose an API according to the device and portability requirement.
functions.longDescription: C++ does not provide a standard serial-port or USB API. Use a cross-platform library or the operating-system API appropriate for the target.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Serial / UART over COM or tty:
   - portable C++ wrapper: Boost.Asio serial_port
   - Windows native API: CreateFile + DCB + ReadFile/WriteFile
   - POSIX native API: open + termios + read/write

 USB device access:
   - generic USB transfers: libusb
   - USB HID or Bluetooth HID: HIDAPI
   - vendor SDK / OS driver API when required by the device
*/

functions.name: Boost.Asio open and configure serial port
functions.description: Open a serial port portably with Boost.Asio.
functions.longDescription: Boost.Asio exposes a portable serial_port abstraction. A Windows name such as COM3 and a POSIX name such as /dev/ttyUSB0 identify the device.

functions.name: Boost.Asio serial write
functions.description: Write a complete serial buffer.
functions.longDescription: Use boost::asio::write when the complete buffer must be sent before continuing. For GUI or concurrent applications, prefer asynchronous operations.

functions.name: Boost.Asio serial read line
functions.description: Read a line-terminated serial frame.
functions.longDescription: read_until is convenient for text protocols. For binary frames, maintain a byte buffer and implement explicit frame extraction.

functions.name: Boost.Asio async serial read_some
functions.description: Start an asynchronous serial read.
functions.longDescription: Asynchronous reads avoid blocking the calling thread. Keep buffers alive until the completion handler runs.

functions.name: Win32 open configure COM port
functions.description: Open and configure a Windows COM port with the Win32 API.
functions.longDescription: CreateFile opens Windows communications resources. The \.\COM12 form also works for COM port numbers greater than 9.

functions.name: Win32 COM timeouts and read write
functions.description: Configure serial timeouts and exchange bytes on Windows.
functions.longDescription: For responsive applications, consider overlapped I/O instead of synchronous ReadFile and WriteFile calls.

functions.name: POSIX open configure tty serial port
functions.description: Open and configure a serial tty on Linux or another POSIX platform.
functions.longDescription: termios is the POSIX native serial configuration API. Add error handling and select, poll, or asynchronous integration according to the application architecture.

functions.name: libusb initialize open VID PID
functions.description: Initialize libusb and open a USB device by vendor and product ID.
functions.longDescription: libusb provides user-space access to USB devices. A production implementation should inspect descriptors, handle kernel-driver ownership where relevant, release interfaces, close the handle, and exit the context.

functions.name: libusb bulk transfer
functions.description: Exchange data through a USB bulk endpoint.
functions.longDescription: The endpoint address determines the direction. Bulk transfers are common for devices exchanging larger reliable byte streams.

functions.name: libusb control transfer
functions.description: Send a USB control transfer.
functions.longDescription: Control transfers are commonly used for setup, vendor commands, and small structured exchanges over endpoint zero.

functions.name: libusb cleanup
functions.description: Release a libusb interface and close the library cleanly.
functions.longDescription: Pair resource acquisition with cleanup paths. RAII wrappers are appropriate when libusb is used throughout a larger C++ application.

functions.name: libusb async transfer note
functions.signature:
/*
 libusb also exposes an asynchronous transfer API:
   libusb_alloc_transfer
   libusb_fill_bulk_transfer / libusb_fill_interrupt_transfer
   libusb_submit_transfer
   libusb_handle_events
   libusb_cancel_transfer
   libusb_free_transfer

 Use it when synchronous transfers would block an event loop or when
 several transfers must remain in flight concurrently.
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 libusb also exposes an asynchronous transfer API:
   libusb_alloc_transfer
   libusb_fill_bulk_transfer / libusb_fill_interrupt_transfer
   libusb_submit_transfer
   libusb_handle_events
   libusb_cancel_transfer
   libusb_free_transfer

 Use it when synchronous transfers would block an event loop or when
 several transfers must remain in flight concurrently.
*/
functions.description: Document when to use asynchronous USB transfers.
functions.longDescription: The asynchronous libusb API is more powerful but also more complex than the synchronous transfer helpers.
functions.declaration:
/*
 libusb also exposes an asynchronous transfer API:
   libusb_alloc_transfer
   libusb_fill_bulk_transfer / libusb_fill_interrupt_transfer
   libusb_submit_transfer
   libusb_handle_events
   libusb_cancel_transfer
   libusb_free_transfer

 Use it when synchronous transfers would block an event loop or when
 several transfers must remain in flight concurrently.
*/

functions.name: HIDAPI open read write
functions.description: Communicate with a USB HID or Bluetooth HID device portably.
functions.longDescription: HIDAPI is appropriate for HID-class USB and Bluetooth devices when direct HID reports are sufficient.

functions.name: Linux enumerate serial tty devices with filesystem
functions.description: List common Linux USB serial device nodes.
functions.longDescription: USB-to-UART adapters commonly expose /dev/ttyUSB* or /dev/ttyACM*. This simple scan is useful for tooling; production discovery can use libudev metadata.

functions.name: Linux libudev enumerate tty devices
functions.description: Enumerate Linux tty devices and inspect vendor/product metadata with libudev.
functions.longDescription: libudev is the Linux-oriented approach when you need stable metadata, hotplug handling, or filtering by USB attributes instead of only scanning /dev.

functions.name: C++ backend frontend architecture
functions.signature:
/*
 Browser frontend (HTML / CSS / JavaScript)
        |
        | fetch() HTTP requests + JSON payloads
        v
 C++ backend REST API
        |
        +-- domain logic
        +-- serial / USB / TCP devices
        +-- configuration files

 Typical endpoints:
   GET  /api/status
   POST /api/config
   POST /api/command
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Browser frontend (HTML / CSS / JavaScript)
        |
        | fetch() HTTP requests + JSON payloads
        v
 C++ backend REST API
        |
        +-- domain logic
        +-- serial / USB / TCP devices
        +-- configuration files

 Typical endpoints:
   GET  /api/status
   POST /api/config
   POST /api/command
*/
functions.description: Outline a practical local or embedded web architecture.
functions.longDescription: A browser cannot call C++ functions directly. Expose an HTTP, WebSocket, or similar network boundary and exchange explicit payloads such as JSON.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Browser frontend (HTML / CSS / JavaScript)
        |
        | fetch() HTTP requests + JSON payloads
        v
 C++ backend REST API
        |
        +-- domain logic
        +-- serial / USB / TCP devices
        +-- configuration files

 Typical endpoints:
   GET  /api/status
   POST /api/config
   POST /api/command
*/

functions.name: Boost.Asio TCP client
functions.description: Connect to a TCP server and exchange bytes.
functions.longDescription: TCP provides a reliable ordered byte stream. Define framing explicitly, for example lines, length prefixes, or a higher-level protocol such as HTTP.

functions.name: Boost.Asio TCP server accept
functions.description: Listen for one incoming TCP connection.
functions.longDescription: A production server typically uses asynchronous accept and per-connection state objects rather than blocking on one client.

functions.name: Boost.Asio UDP send receive
functions.description: Send a datagram and receive a response.
functions.longDescription: UDP exchanges independent datagrams without TCP ordering or delivery guarantees. It suits telemetry, discovery, and controlled low-latency protocols.

functions.name: libcurl HTTP GET capture string
functions.description: Perform an HTTP GET request and collect the response body.
functions.longDescription: libcurl is a mature transfer library. The easy interface is synchronous; use the multi interface when several transfers or event-loop integration are required.

functions.name: libcurl POST JSON
functions.description: Send a JSON request body with libcurl.
functions.longDescription: For HTTPS, configure certificate verification correctly. Do not disable peer or host verification in production code.

functions.name: libcurl easy versus multi reference
functions.signature:
/*
 libcurl easy interface:
   curl_easy_init
   curl_easy_setopt
   curl_easy_perform      // blocking transfer
   curl_easy_cleanup

 libcurl multi interface:
   curl_multi_init
   curl_multi_add_handle
   curl_multi_perform
   curl_multi_poll
   curl_multi_remove_handle
   curl_multi_cleanup
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 libcurl easy interface:
   curl_easy_init
   curl_easy_setopt
   curl_easy_perform      // blocking transfer
   curl_easy_cleanup

 libcurl multi interface:
   curl_multi_init
   curl_multi_add_handle
   curl_multi_perform
   curl_multi_poll
   curl_multi_remove_handle
   curl_multi_cleanup
*/
functions.description: Choose between synchronous and asynchronous-style libcurl interfaces.
functions.longDescription: The easy interface is appropriate for simple blocking requests. The multi interface allows several transfers and application-driven polling in a single thread.
functions.declaration:
/*
 libcurl easy interface:
   curl_easy_init
   curl_easy_setopt
   curl_easy_perform      // blocking transfer
   curl_easy_cleanup

 libcurl multi interface:
   curl_multi_init
   curl_multi_add_handle
   curl_multi_perform
   curl_multi_poll
   curl_multi_remove_handle
   curl_multi_cleanup
*/

functions.name: cpp-httplib REST GET endpoint
functions.description: Expose a JSON status endpoint from a small C++ HTTP server.
functions.longDescription: cpp-httplib is a compact third-party header-only HTTP library. It is convenient for local tools, prototypes, and moderate HTTP services.

functions.name: cpp-httplib REST POST JSON endpoint
functions.description: Parse a JSON command received from a JavaScript frontend.
functions.longDescription: Validate payloads and return explicit HTTP status codes. Do not trust data received from a browser or another network client.

functions.name: cpp-httplib static frontend and CORS
functions.description: Serve static frontend files and add a CORS header when needed.
functions.longDescription: For development, the frontend and backend may use different local ports and require CORS headers. For production, serve a built frontend from the same origin when practical.

functions.name: cpp-httplib server thread and stop
functions.description: Run a small HTTP server on a dedicated thread and stop it cleanly.
functions.longDescription: Keep shutdown explicit. Integrate error reporting and ownership rules if the server shares application state with GUI or device threads.

functions.name: browser fetch GET status
functions.description: Fetch a C++ backend status endpoint from JavaScript.
functions.longDescription: This JavaScript counterpart shows the browser side of a C++ REST backend. The frontend consumes JSON rather than linking directly to C++ code.

functions.name: browser fetch POST command
functions.description: Send a JSON command from JavaScript to a C++ backend.
functions.longDescription: Use explicit REST endpoints for user actions, settings updates, and device commands.

functions.name: Boost.Beast WebSocket server session skeleton
functions.description: Accept and echo one WebSocket message with Boost.Beast.
functions.longDescription: WebSockets are suitable for live telemetry, notifications, and bidirectional updates. Boost.Beast builds HTTP and WebSocket primitives on Boost.Asio.

functions.name: communication technology selection quick reference
functions.signature:
/*
 UART / serial       simple point-to-point byte stream; define framing
 USB CDC serial      appears as COM or tty; use the serial API
 USB HID             HIDAPI for HID reports
 Generic USB         libusb or a vendor SDK
 Ethernet / Wi-Fi    TCP, UDP, HTTP, WebSocket after network connection exists
 Bluetooth Classic   RFCOMM / sockets or platform SDK
 Bluetooth Low Energy GATT APIs through platform SDK or a BLE library
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 UART / serial       simple point-to-point byte stream; define framing
 USB CDC serial      appears as COM or tty; use the serial API
 USB HID             HIDAPI for HID reports
 Generic USB         libusb or a vendor SDK
 Ethernet / Wi-Fi    TCP, UDP, HTTP, WebSocket after network connection exists
 Bluetooth Classic   RFCOMM / sockets or platform SDK
 Bluetooth Low Energy GATT APIs through platform SDK or a BLE library
*/
functions.description: Choose the transport and protocol layer deliberately.
functions.longDescription: Separate the physical transport from the application protocol. For example, Wi-Fi can carry the same HTTP or TCP protocol as wired Ethernet.
functions.declaration:
/*
 UART / serial       simple point-to-point byte stream; define framing
 USB CDC serial      appears as COM or tty; use the serial API
 USB HID             HIDAPI for HID reports
 Generic USB         libusb or a vendor SDK
 Ethernet / Wi-Fi    TCP, UDP, HTTP, WebSocket after network connection exists
 Bluetooth Classic   RFCOMM / sockets or platform SDK
 Bluetooth Low Energy GATT APIs through platform SDK or a BLE library
*/

functions.name: C++ standard connectivity scope note
functions.signature:
/*
 The C++ standard library does not configure Wi-Fi adapters, pair Bluetooth
 devices, open serial ports, or perform raw USB transfers.

 Use:
   - portable libraries such as Boost.Asio, libcurl, libusb, HIDAPI
   - operating-system APIs such as Win32 COM, Native Wifi, Winsock Bluetooth,
     POSIX termios, or BlueZ
   - vendor SDKs when a device requires a custom driver or protocol
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 The C++ standard library does not configure Wi-Fi adapters, pair Bluetooth
 devices, open serial ports, or perform raw USB transfers.

 Use:
   - portable libraries such as Boost.Asio, libcurl, libusb, HIDAPI
   - operating-system APIs such as Win32 COM, Native Wifi, Winsock Bluetooth,
     POSIX termios, or BlueZ
   - vendor SDKs when a device requires a custom driver or protocol
*/
functions.description: Document which communication layers are outside the C++ standard library.
functions.longDescription: Keeping platform-specific code behind narrow interfaces makes the rest of the application easier to test and port.
functions.declaration:
/*
 The C++ standard library does not configure Wi-Fi adapters, pair Bluetooth
 devices, open serial ports, or perform raw USB transfers.

 Use:
   - portable libraries such as Boost.Asio, libcurl, libusb, HIDAPI
   - operating-system APIs such as Win32 COM, Native Wifi, Winsock Bluetooth,
     POSIX termios, or BlueZ
   - vendor SDKs when a device requires a custom driver or protocol
*/

functions.name: newline delimited UART protocol
functions.description: Build and parse a simple line-oriented serial protocol.
functions.longDescription: Line protocols are easy to debug manually. For arbitrary binary payloads or high throughput, use explicit binary framing.

functions.name: length prefixed binary frame
functions.description: Define a compact framed binary message.
functions.longDescription: Define byte order, size limits, CRC coverage, and resynchronization behavior explicitly. Do not transmit compiler-dependent object layouts without a wire-format contract.

functions.name: CRC16 Modbus helper
functions.description: Calculate a CRC-16 suitable for many serial protocols.
functions.longDescription: Use the CRC polynomial and initialization required by the actual protocol. CRC detects accidental corruption; it is not a security mechanism.

functions.name: byte stream accumulator parser
functions.description: Accumulate partial reads before extracting complete frames.
functions.longDescription: Serial ports and TCP sockets are byte streams: one read does not necessarily correspond to one message. Accumulate bytes and parse complete frames explicitly.

functions.name: Wi-Fi uses regular IP sockets after connection
functions.signature:
/*
 Once the operating system has joined a Wi-Fi network, application traffic
 normally uses the same IP APIs as Ethernet:
   - Boost.Asio TCP / UDP sockets
   - libcurl HTTP client
   - cpp-httplib or Boost.Beast server
   - WebSocket for live bidirectional updates
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Once the operating system has joined a Wi-Fi network, application traffic
 normally uses the same IP APIs as Ethernet:
   - Boost.Asio TCP / UDP sockets
   - libcurl HTTP client
   - cpp-httplib or Boost.Beast server
   - WebSocket for live bidirectional updates
*/
functions.description: Use TCP, UDP, HTTP, or WebSocket normally once the operating system is connected to Wi-Fi.
functions.longDescription: Wi-Fi association and IP application traffic are different layers. Many applications only need the regular socket layer.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Once the operating system has joined a Wi-Fi network, application traffic
 normally uses the same IP APIs as Ethernet:
   - Boost.Asio TCP / UDP sockets
   - libcurl HTTP client
   - cpp-httplib or Boost.Beast server
   - WebSocket for live bidirectional updates
*/

functions.name: Windows Native Wifi enumerate interfaces
functions.description: Enumerate enabled Wi-Fi adapters through the Windows Native Wifi API.
functions.longDescription: Native Wifi is the Windows API for enumerating interfaces, scanning, inspecting profiles, and managing WLAN state. Free buffers returned by WLAN functions with WlanFreeMemory.

functions.name: Windows Native Wifi scan available networks note
functions.signature:
/*
 Windows Native Wifi scan flow:
   1. WlanOpenHandle
   2. WlanEnumInterfaces
   3. WlanRegisterNotification for WLAN_NOTIFICATION_SOURCE_ACM
   4. WlanScan(interfaceGuid, ...)
   5. wait for wlan_notification_acm_scan_complete or timeout
   6. WlanGetAvailableNetworkList
   7. WlanFreeMemory for returned lists
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Windows Native Wifi scan flow:
   1. WlanOpenHandle
   2. WlanEnumInterfaces
   3. WlanRegisterNotification for WLAN_NOTIFICATION_SOURCE_ACM
   4. WlanScan(interfaceGuid, ...)
   5. wait for wlan_notification_acm_scan_complete or timeout
   6. WlanGetAvailableNetworkList
   7. WlanFreeMemory for returned lists
*/
functions.description: Document the asynchronous WLAN scan flow on Windows.
functions.longDescription: WlanScan returns immediately. Treat scanning as an asynchronous system operation rather than assuming the result list is updated before the call returns.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Windows Native Wifi scan flow:
   1. WlanOpenHandle
   2. WlanEnumInterfaces
   3. WlanRegisterNotification for WLAN_NOTIFICATION_SOURCE_ACM
   4. WlanScan(interfaceGuid, ...)
   5. wait for wlan_notification_acm_scan_complete or timeout
   6. WlanGetAvailableNetworkList
   7. WlanFreeMemory for returned lists
*/

functions.name: Linux NetworkManager D-Bus Wi-Fi control note
functions.description: Use NetworkManager D-Bus or libnm for Linux Wi-Fi discovery and connection management.
functions.longDescription: NetworkManager exposes programmatic D-Bus interfaces for network management. For application data after association, use normal IP sockets. Use the management API only when the program must scan, select, or reconfigure Wi-Fi links.

functions.name: Linux nl80211 low-level Wi-Fi note
functions.description: Reference the Linux nl80211 interface for low-level wireless tooling.
functions.longDescription: nl80211 is lower-level than NetworkManager. It is appropriate for specialized wireless tooling but usually unnecessary for an application that only exchanges data over an established Wi-Fi connection.

functions.name: Bluetooth API selection quick reference
functions.signature:
/*
 Bluetooth HID device:
   - HIDAPI may be sufficient

 Bluetooth Classic RFCOMM / custom sockets:
   - Windows: Winsock Bluetooth (AF_BTH)
   - Linux: BlueZ socket APIs or a higher-level library

 Bluetooth Low Energy:
   - use GATT APIs through the platform SDK or a BLE library
   - model services, characteristics, notifications, and pairing explicitly
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 Bluetooth HID device:
   - HIDAPI may be sufficient

 Bluetooth Classic RFCOMM / custom sockets:
   - Windows: Winsock Bluetooth (AF_BTH)
   - Linux: BlueZ socket APIs or a higher-level library

 Bluetooth Low Energy:
   - use GATT APIs through the platform SDK or a BLE library
   - model services, characteristics, notifications, and pairing explicitly
*/
functions.description: Choose a Bluetooth path according to the profile and platform.
functions.longDescription: Bluetooth is not one generic byte stream API. The appropriate interface depends on the profile: HID, RFCOMM, or BLE GATT.
functions.declaration:
/*
 Bluetooth HID device:
   - HIDAPI may be sufficient

 Bluetooth Classic RFCOMM / custom sockets:
   - Windows: Winsock Bluetooth (AF_BTH)
   - Linux: BlueZ socket APIs or a higher-level library

 Bluetooth Low Energy:
   - use GATT APIs through the platform SDK or a BLE library
   - model services, characteristics, notifications, and pairing explicitly
*/

functions.name: Windows Winsock Bluetooth RFCOMM socket skeleton
functions.description: Open a Bluetooth RFCOMM socket on Windows.
functions.longDescription: This is a Windows-specific Bluetooth Classic RFCOMM outline. Add discovery, pairing, error handling, and lifetime management for a production implementation.

functions.name: Linux BlueZ RFCOMM socket skeleton
functions.description: Open a Bluetooth RFCOMM socket through Linux BlueZ headers.
functions.longDescription: This is a Linux-specific Bluetooth Classic RFCOMM outline. BLE uses a different GATT-oriented model.

functions.name: BLE GATT conceptual flow
functions.signature:
/*
 BLE GATT client flow:
   1. discover or select peripheral
   2. pair / bond if required
   3. connect
   4. discover services
   5. discover characteristics
   6. read / write characteristic values
   7. subscribe to notifications or indications
   8. disconnect cleanly

 Implement with the target platform SDK or a BLE library.
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 BLE GATT client flow:
   1. discover or select peripheral
   2. pair / bond if required
   3. connect
   4. discover services
   5. discover characteristics
   6. read / write characteristic values
   7. subscribe to notifications or indications
   8. disconnect cleanly

 Implement with the target platform SDK or a BLE library.
*/
functions.description: Document the BLE service and characteristic workflow.
functions.longDescription: BLE communication is structured around GATT services and characteristics rather than a generic serial byte stream.
functions.declaration:
/*
 BLE GATT client flow:
   1. discover or select peripheral
   2. pair / bond if required
   3. connect
   4. discover services
   5. discover characteristics
   6. read / write characteristic values
   7. subscribe to notifications or indications
   8. disconnect cleanly

 Implement with the target platform SDK or a BLE library.
*/

functions.name: Linux BlueZ D-Bus BLE GATT note
functions.description: Use BlueZ D-Bus APIs for Linux BLE discovery, connection, and GATT operations.
functions.longDescription: For BLE on Linux, BlueZ exposes adapter, device, and GATT objects over D-Bus. RFCOMM remains relevant for Bluetooth Classic serial-style communication.

functions.name: popen / _popen command pipe
functions.description: Capture output from an external process.
functions.longDescription: Portable enough for many tooling scenarios when you need to capture stdout from another process.

functions.name: Windows named pipe client
functions.description: Connect to a Windows named pipe from C++.
functions.longDescription: Useful for local IPC on Windows between tools, GUIs, services, or test benches.

functions.name: POSIX pipe / read / write
functions.description: Basic anonymous pipe example on Linux or POSIX.
functions.longDescription: A small POSIX pipe example showing low-level IPC primitives.

functions.name: POSIX mkfifo named pipe
functions.description: Create and open a POSIX named pipe for local process communication.
functions.longDescription: A FIFO is simple for local unidirectional or loosely coupled exchanges. For request-response protocols, Unix domain sockets are usually easier to structure.

functions.name: Unix domain socket server skeleton
functions.description: Create a local stream server with AF_UNIX sockets.
functions.longDescription: Unix domain sockets provide local stream or datagram communication with familiar socket semantics and filesystem-based addressing.

functions.name: POSIX shared memory shm_open mmap
functions.description: Share a memory region between local processes with shm_open and mmap.
functions.longDescription: Shared memory is efficient but requires an explicit synchronization protocol, versioned layout, and careful lifecycle handling.

functions.name: Linux epoll event loop skeleton
functions.description: Wait on several Linux descriptors efficiently with epoll.
functions.longDescription: epoll is Linux-specific and useful when coordinating sockets, pipes, inotify descriptors, and device descriptors in one event loop.

functions.name: Linux getifaddrs enumerate network interfaces
functions.description: Enumerate Linux network interfaces and IPv4 addresses with getifaddrs.
functions.longDescription: getifaddrs is useful for interface enumeration, diagnostics, and selecting a bind address on Linux and other POSIX-like systems.

groups.name: Threads & lifecycle
groups.description: Thread creation, joining, cooperative stop requests, and worker lifetime.

functions.name: std::thread worker
functions.description: Launch and join a worker thread.
functions.longDescription: Basic pattern for running work concurrently. Always join or detach every std::thread.

groups.name: Mutexes & locking
groups.description: Mutual exclusion and RAII-based locking for shared state.

functions.name: std::mutex with lock_guard
functions.description: Protect shared data with a mutex.
functions.longDescription: RAII locking with std::lock_guard keeps locking safe and exception-friendly.

groups.name: Condition variables & producer-consumer
groups.description: Blocking waits and producer-consumer synchronization patterns.

functions.name: condition_variable producer/consumer
functions.description: Wait for work using a condition variable.
functions.longDescription: Use when one thread must sleep until another thread produces data or a stop flag changes.

groups.name: Atomics & memory ordering
groups.description: Atomic state, lock-free updates, and publication ordering.

functions.name: std::atomic flag
functions.description: Lock-free shared flag between threads.
functions.longDescription: Atomic flags are useful for simple stop requests or low-contention shared state.

functions.name: chrono duration literals
functions.description: Use readable chrono literals such as ms, s, and min.
functions.longDescription: C++14 chrono literals make durations explicit and type-safe. They avoid fragile raw integer delays whose unit is unclear.

functions.name: std::this_thread::sleep_for
functions.description: Pause the current thread for a relative duration.
functions.longDescription: sleep_for blocks the current thread for at least the requested relative duration. It is simple and portable, but it should not be used on a UI thread or in a latency-critical loop.

functions.name: std::this_thread::sleep_until
functions.description: Pause the current thread until an absolute deadline.
functions.longDescription: sleep_until waits until an absolute time point. It is preferable to repeated sleep_for calls in periodic loops because it limits accumulated drift.

functions.name: measure elapsed time with steady_clock
functions.description: Measure execution time with a monotonic clock.
functions.longDescription: Use steady_clock for benchmarking, timeouts, and elapsed-time measurements. Unlike system_clock, a monotonic clock is not affected by wall-clock corrections.

functions.name: duration_cast conversion
functions.description: Convert a chrono duration to a specific unit.
functions.longDescription: duration_cast performs explicit chrono unit conversion. Calling count() returns the numeric representation in the selected unit.

functions.name: deadline timeout check
functions.description: Check whether a monotonic deadline has expired.
functions.longDescription: Represent a timeout as an absolute steady_clock deadline. This is more robust than incrementing counters or repeatedly subtracting approximate delays.

functions.name: periodic loop without accumulated drift
functions.description: Run a periodic loop using an absolute next deadline.
functions.longDescription: Advance the next deadline by one fixed period and sleep until that absolute time point. This avoids the cumulative drift caused by sleeping for a full period after each iteration.

functions.name: condition_variable wait_for timeout
functions.description: Wait for a condition with a relative timeout.
functions.longDescription: wait_for blocks efficiently until notified, until the predicate becomes true, or until the relative timeout expires. Prefer it to active polling when another thread can signal readiness.

functions.name: condition_variable wait_until deadline
functions.description: Wait for a condition until an absolute deadline.
functions.longDescription: wait_until is convenient when several wait operations must share one absolute timeout budget.

functions.name: one-shot delayed task
functions.description: Run an operation once after a delay in a managed worker thread.
functions.longDescription: std::jthread is a C++20 managed thread that joins automatically when destroyed. This pattern runs one delayed action without leaking or forgetting to join a std::thread.

functions.name: cancellable periodic timer with jthread
functions.description: Run a periodic callback until a C++20 stop request is received.
functions.longDescription: std::jthread and std::stop_token provide a clean C++20 cancellation model. The absolute next deadline prevents periodic drift.

functions.name: reusable Stopwatch class
functions.description: Encapsulate elapsed-time measurement in a small utility class.
functions.longDescription: A reusable monotonic stopwatch is useful for instrumentation, profiling, communication timeouts, and test-bench diagnostics.

functions.name: system_clock wall-clock timestamp
functions.description: Convert the current wall-clock time to time_t for timestamps.
functions.longDescription: Use system_clock for calendar timestamps and logs. Do not use it for elapsed-time measurements because wall-clock time may jump after synchronization or manual adjustment.

functions.name: std::this_thread::yield
functions.signature:
#include <thread>

std::this_thread::yield();
functions.description: Hint that the current thread can let another runnable thread execute.
functions.longDescription: yield is only a scheduler hint. It is not a delay and should not replace sleep_for, condition variables, or proper synchronization.
functions.declaration:
#include <thread>

std::this_thread::yield();

functions.name: standard timer design reference
functions.signature:
/* C++ timing quick reference
   Relative blocking delay       : std::this_thread::sleep_for(duration)
   Absolute blocking deadline    : std::this_thread::sleep_until(timePoint)
   Elapsed time / timeout clock  : std::chrono::steady_clock
   Calendar timestamp clock      : std::chrono::system_clock
   Signaled wait with timeout    : std::condition_variable::wait_for / wait_until
   Managed cancellable worker    : std::jthread + std::stop_token (C++20)

   The standard library does not provide a universal event-loop timer object.
   GUI frameworks such as Qt and operating systems expose their own event timers.
*/
functions.insertText:
/* C++ timing quick reference
   Relative blocking delay       : std::this_thread::sleep_for(duration)
   Absolute blocking deadline    : std::this_thread::sleep_until(timePoint)
   Elapsed time / timeout clock  : std::chrono::steady_clock
   Calendar timestamp clock      : std::chrono::system_clock
   Signaled wait with timeout    : std::condition_variable::wait_for / wait_until
   Managed cancellable worker    : std::jthread + std::stop_token (C++20)

   The standard library does not provide a universal event-loop timer object.
   GUI frameworks such as Qt and operating systems expose their own event timers.
*/
functions.description: Choose the appropriate timing primitive for the required behavior.
functions.longDescription: This reference clarifies which standard primitive matches each timing need. For GUI applications, prefer the framework event loop timer, such as QTimer, rather than blocking the UI thread.
functions.declaration:
/* C++ timing quick reference
   Relative blocking delay       : std::this_thread::sleep_for(duration)
   Absolute blocking deadline    : std::this_thread::sleep_until(timePoint)
   Elapsed time / timeout clock  : std::chrono::steady_clock
   Calendar timestamp clock      : std::chrono::system_clock
   Signaled wait with timeout    : std::condition_variable::wait_for / wait_until
   Managed cancellable worker    : std::jthread + std::stop_token (C++20)

   The standard library does not provide a universal event-loop timer object.
   GUI frameworks such as Qt and operating systems expose their own event timers.
*/

functions.name: new / delete
functions.description: Raw dynamic allocation in C++.
functions.longDescription: Use when needed, but prefer standard containers or smart pointers in modern C++.

functions.name: new[] / delete[]
functions.description: Allocate a raw dynamic array.
functions.longDescription: Prefer std::vector for resizable arrays, but this pattern is still useful to understand older codebases.

functions.name: std::unique_ptr
functions.description: Single-owner smart pointer.
functions.longDescription: Preferred ownership model when one object clearly owns the resource.

functions.name: std::shared_ptr
functions.description: Reference-counted shared ownership.
functions.longDescription: Useful when multiple components truly share lifetime, but avoid it when ownership can stay simple.

functions.name: Custom deleter
functions.description: unique_ptr with a custom cleanup function.
functions.longDescription: Great for wrapping C handles inside RAII-managed smart pointers.

functions.name: RAII wrapper
functions.description: Resource wrapper releasing automatically in the destructor.
functions.longDescription: RAII is one of the most important C++ idioms for safe resource management.

groups.name: Raw pointers & const qualifiers
groups.description: Raw pointers, double indirection, and const qualification rules.

functions.name: raw pointer T *
functions.description: Declare a raw pointer.
functions.longDescription: Use raw pointers mainly as non-owning observers, optional references, low-level handles or C interoperability boundaries.

functions.name: pointer to const: const T *
functions.description: Reseatable pointer that cannot modify its pointee.
functions.longDescription: Read from right to left: pointerToConst is a pointer to a const int.

functions.name: const pointer: T * const
functions.description: Fixed pointer that can modify its pointee.
functions.longDescription: Read from right to left: constPointer is a const pointer to an int.

functions.name: const pointer to const: const T * const
functions.description: Fixed pointer to a read-only pointee.
functions.longDescription: Both levels are const: the object is read-only through this pointer and the pointer variable cannot be reseated.

functions.name: pointer const reading rule
functions.signature:
/*
 T *p;               pointer to T
 const T *p;         pointer to const T
 T const *p;         pointer to const T
 T * const p;        const pointer to T
 const T * const p;  const pointer to const T
 T **p;              pointer to pointer to T
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 T *p;               pointer to T
 const T *p;         pointer to const T
 T const *p;         pointer to const T
 T * const p;        const pointer to T
 const T * const p;  const pointer to const T
 T **p;              pointer to pointer to T
*/
functions.description: Reference guide for reading const pointer declarations.
functions.longDescription: Read declarations from the variable name outward.
functions.declaration:
/*
 T *p;               pointer to T
 const T *p;         pointer to const T
 T const *p;         pointer to const T
 T * const p;        const pointer to T
 const T * const p;  const pointer to const T
 T **p;              pointer to pointer to T
*/

functions.name: pointer to pointer: T **
functions.description: Use double indirection, often for C out parameters.
functions.longDescription: When you control a modern C++ interface, prefer returning a container or smart pointer.

groups.name: References & observers
groups.description: References, rvalue references, and non-owning observer patterns.

functions.name: reference and const reference
functions.signature:
void Update(Device &device);
void Print(const Device &device);
functions.description: Use references for mandatory aliases.
functions.longDescription: A reference normally expresses a required non-null object. const T & avoids copying while blocking writes through the reference.
functions.declaration:
void Update(Device &device);
void Print(const Device &device);

functions.name: rvalue reference T &&
functions.description: Consume movable temporary objects.
functions.longDescription: Rvalue references underpin move construction, move assignment and consuming overloads.

functions.name: non-owning observer raw pointer
functions.description: Document a nullable pointer that does not own lifetime.
functions.longDescription: Use a reference instead when null is forbidden.

groups.name: Smart pointers & ownership
groups.description: RAII ownership with unique_ptr, shared_ptr, and weak_ptr.

functions.name: std::unique_ptr with std::make_unique
functions.description: Represent exclusive ownership.
functions.longDescription: std::unique_ptr is the preferred default owning pointer. It moves but does not copy.

functions.name: std::unique_ptr<T[]> array
functions.description: Own a fixed-size dynamic array.
functions.longDescription: Prefer std::vector for general resizable sequences.

functions.name: transfer std::unique_ptr ownership
functions.description: Move exclusive ownership.
functions.longDescription: After std::move, the source unique_ptr remains valid but is normally empty.

functions.name: std::shared_ptr with std::make_shared
functions.description: Represent genuine shared lifetime ownership.
functions.longDescription: Use shared_ptr only when several components truly own the same lifetime.

functions.name: std::weak_ptr lock
functions.description: Observe shared state without extending lifetime.
functions.longDescription: weak_ptr avoids ownership extension and can break cycles.

functions.name: break shared_ptr cycle with weak_ptr
functions.description: Use weak_ptr for a non-owning back-reference.
functions.longDescription: A pair of mutual shared_ptr references would prevent destruction because neither count reaches zero.

functions.name: const smart pointer vs smart pointer to const
functions.description: Distinguish wrapper constness from pointee constness.
functions.longDescription: std::unique_ptr<const T> owns a read-only T. const std::unique_ptr<T> freezes reset/move on that variable while T remains mutable.

functions.name: smart pointer selection reference
functions.signature:
/*
 std::unique_ptr<T> : one owner; preferred owning pointer
 std::shared_ptr<T> : several genuine owners
 std::weak_ptr<T>   : observer of shared_ptr-managed object
 T *                : optional non-owning observer / interop
 T &                : mandatory non-owning alias
 std::vector<T>     : preferred resizable owned sequence
*/
functions.insertText:
/*
 std::unique_ptr<T> : one owner; preferred owning pointer
 std::shared_ptr<T> : several genuine owners
 std::weak_ptr<T>   : observer of shared_ptr-managed object
 T *                : optional non-owning observer / interop
 T &                : mandatory non-owning alias
 std::vector<T>     : preferred resizable owned sequence
*/
functions.description: Choose the ownership representation.
functions.longDescription: Start with values, containers and unique_ptr. Introduce shared_ptr only when the ownership graph needs it.
functions.declaration:
/*
 std::unique_ptr<T> : one owner; preferred owning pointer
 std::shared_ptr<T> : several genuine owners
 std::weak_ptr<T>   : observer of shared_ptr-managed object
 T *                : optional non-owning observer / interop
 T &                : mandatory non-owning alias
 std::vector<T>     : preferred resizable owned sequence
*/

functions.longDescription: Pointers to member functions are distinct from ordinary function pointers because they require an object instance. They are useful in dispatch tables, state machines, and callback binding helpers.

functions.name: QLibrary load DLL beside executable
functions.signature: QLibrary load DLL beside executable
functions.description: Load a runtime library stored next to the Qt executable and report a precise error.
functions.longDescription: Resolve the absolute runtime path from applicationDirPath() when the DLL or shared object is deployed beside the executable. Always log QLibrary::errorString() when loading fails.
functions.declaration: QLibrary load DLL beside executable

functions.name: QLibrary resolve typed C export
functions.signature: QLibrary resolve typed C export
functions.description: Resolve an exported C symbol from a dynamically loaded DLL or shared object.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary::resolve() returns a raw symbol pointer. Convert it to a function-pointer type that exactly matches the exported ABI. A C-compatible exported function avoids C++ name mangling.
functions.declaration: QLibrary resolve typed C export

functions.name: QLibrary validate mandatory symbol table
functions.signature: QLibrary validate mandatory symbol table
functions.description: Resolve several mandatory exports and reject an incomplete DLL early.
functions.longDescription: Resolve the complete required API before starting work. This prevents partially initialized applications when one expected DLL symbol is missing or renamed.
functions.declaration: QLibrary validate mandatory symbol table

functions.name: QLibrary static resolve convenience
functions.signature: QLibrary static resolve convenience
functions.description: Resolve one exported symbol without keeping a QLibrary instance explicitly.
functions.longDescription: The static QLibrary::resolve() helper is concise for a single symbol. Use an explicit QLibrary object when you need diagnostics, multiple exports, lifecycle control, or a reusable loader class.
functions.declaration: QLibrary static resolve convenience

functions.name: Cross-platform shared-library file name
functions.signature: Cross-platform shared-library file name
functions.description: Select a runtime shared-library name for Windows, Linux, and macOS.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary is cross-platform, but deployed file names conventionally differ between Windows DLLs, Linux shared objects, and macOS dynamic libraries.
functions.declaration: Cross-platform shared-library file name

functions.name: QLibrary reusable loader class skeleton
functions.signature: QLibrary reusable loader class skeleton
functions.description: Wrap QLibrary and resolved function pointers inside a small reusable loader class.
functions.longDescription: A dedicated loader class centralizes path resolution, exported prototypes, diagnostics, and API validation. Keep the QLibrary object alive while any resolved function pointer may still be used.
functions.declaration: QLibrary reusable loader class skeleton

functions.name: Export C ABI for QLibrary resolve
functions.signature: Export C ABI for QLibrary resolve
functions.description: Export a stable runtime-loadable C function from a Windows DLL or Linux shared object.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary::resolve() works most predictably with a stable C ABI. On Windows export the symbol with __declspec(dllexport); on GCC/Clang builds, explicitly requesting default visibility is useful when hidden visibility is enabled.
functions.declaration: Export C ABI for QLibrary resolve

functions.name: QLibrary unload lifecycle note
functions.signature: QLibrary unload lifecycle note
functions.insertText:
/*
 Keep the QLibrary object alive as long as resolved function pointers are used.
 After unload(), every resolved pointer must be considered invalid.
 unload() can fail if another QLibrary instance still references the same library.
 For process-wide driver DLLs, keeping the library loaded until shutdown is often safer.
*/
functions.description: Document the lifetime rules for resolved symbols and unloading.
functions.longDescription: Dynamic-library lifetime bugs are difficult to diagnose. Avoid calling a resolved function after unloading the corresponding library.
functions.declaration: QLibrary unload lifecycle note

functions.name: QLibrary versus QPluginLoader decision
functions.signature: QLibrary versus QPluginLoader decision
functions.description: Choose between raw exported functions and a Qt plugin object.
functions.longDescription: QLibrary provides explicit runtime symbol resolution. QPluginLoader is intended for Qt plugins and returns a QObject root instance.
functions.declaration: QLibrary versus QPluginLoader decision

functions.name: QPluginLoader interface skeleton
functions.signature: QPluginLoader interface skeleton
functions.description: Load a Qt plugin DLL and cast its QObject instance to a declared interface.
functions.longDescription: A QPluginLoader-based plugin exports a Qt plugin object rather than individual C symbols. The plugin interface should be declared with Q_DECLARE_INTERFACE and implemented with Q_INTERFACES.
functions.declaration: QPluginLoader interface skeleton

functions.name: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.signature: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.insertText:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)

project(BLE_Sniffeur VERSION 0.1 LANGUAGES CXX)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Widgets)
qt_standard_project_setup()

qt_add_executable(BLE_Sniffeur
    main.cpp
    mainwindow.cpp
    mainwindow.h
    mainwindow.ui
)

target_sources(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE
    bledllloader.cpp
    bledllloader.h
    bledevice.h
)

target_link_libraries(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE Qt6::Widgets)

set(BLE_DLL_SOURCE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")
if(EXISTS "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET BLE_Sniffeur POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:BLE_Sniffeur>/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll"
        COMMENT "Copy Scan_BLE_DLL.dll beside the executable"
    )
else()
    message(WARNING "Scan_BLE_DLL.dll is missing from the project root")
endif()

set_target_properties(BLE_Sniffeur PROPERTIES
    MACOSX_BUNDLE TRUE
    WIN32_EXECUTABLE TRUE
)

include(GNUInstallDirs)
install(TARGETS BLE_Sniffeur
    BUNDLE DESTINATION .
    LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
)
functions.description: Complete Qt Widgets CMakeLists.txt with sources, UI file, dynamic DLL copy, target properties, and installation.
functions.longDescription: A practical baseline matching a Qt Widgets application that loads a driver DLL dynamically with QLibrary. No import .lib is needed for symbols resolved exclusively at runtime.
functions.declaration: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL

if(EXISTS "${DRIVER_DLL}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${DRIVER_DLL}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:MyQtApp>/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll"

functions.insertText:
if(WIN32)
    set(DRIVER_FILE "Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")
elseif(APPLE)
    set(DRIVER_FILE "libScan_BLE_DLL.dylib")
else()
    set(DRIVER_FILE "libScan_BLE_DLL.so")
endif()

set(DRIVER_SOURCE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/${DRIVER_FILE}")
if(EXISTS "${DRIVER_SOURCE}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${DRIVER_SOURCE}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:MyQtApp>/${DRIVER_FILE}"
    )
endif()

functions.name: QLibrary unload lifecycle note
functions.signature: QLibrary unload lifecycle note
functions.insertText:
/*
 Keep the QLibrary object alive as long as resolved function pointers are used.
 After unload(), every resolved pointer must be considered invalid.
 unload() can fail if another QLibrary instance still references the same library.
 For process-wide driver DLLs, keeping the library loaded until shutdown is often safer.
*/
functions.description: Document the lifetime rules for resolved symbols and unloading.
functions.longDescription: Dynamic-library lifetime bugs are difficult to diagnose. Avoid calling a resolved function after unloading the corresponding library.
functions.declaration: QLibrary unload lifecycle note

functions.name: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.signature: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.insertText:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)

project(BLE_Sniffeur VERSION 0.1 LANGUAGES CXX)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Widgets)
qt_standard_project_setup()

qt_add_executable(BLE_Sniffeur
    main.cpp
    mainwindow.cpp
    mainwindow.h
    mainwindow.ui
)

target_sources(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE
    bledllloader.cpp
    bledllloader.h
    bledevice.h
)

target_link_libraries(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE Qt6::Widgets)

set(BLE_DLL_SOURCE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")
if(EXISTS "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET BLE_Sniffeur POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:BLE_Sniffeur>/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll"
        COMMENT "Copy Scan_BLE_DLL.dll beside the executable"
    )
else()
    message(WARNING "Scan_BLE_DLL.dll is missing from the project root")
endif()

set_target_properties(BLE_Sniffeur PROPERTIES
    MACOSX_BUNDLE TRUE
    WIN32_EXECUTABLE TRUE
)

include(GNUInstallDirs)
install(TARGETS BLE_Sniffeur
    BUNDLE DESTINATION .
    LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
)
functions.description: Complete Qt Widgets CMakeLists.txt with sources, UI file, dynamic DLL copy, target properties, and installation.
functions.longDescription: A practical baseline matching a Qt Widgets application that loads a driver DLL dynamically with QLibrary. No import .lib is needed for symbols resolved exclusively at runtime.
functions.declaration: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL

    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different

endif()

    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${DRIVER_SOURCE}"
